1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: SAD. I CALL to witness the admonishing Qur'an.

1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: Saad* – By oath of the renowned Qur’an, (Alphabet of the Arabic language; Allah and to whomever He reveals, know their precise meanings.)

1.author: Arberry
1.text: Sad. By the Koran, containing the Remembrance --

1.author: Daryabadi
1.text: Sad. By the Qur'an full of admonition.

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: Sad [These letters (Sad etc.) are one of the miracles of the Quran and none but Allah (Alone) knows their meanings]. By the Quran full of reminding.

1.author: Maududi
1.text: Sad, and by the Qur'an full of exhortation!

1.author: Mubarakpuri
1.text: Sad. By the Qur'an full of reminding.

1.author: Qarai
1.text: Suad. By the Quran bearing the Reminder,

1.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
1.text: Saad, by the Holy Reading (Koran) of the Remembrance.

1.author: Saheeh International
1.text: Sad. By the Qur'an containing reminder...

1.author: Sarwar
1.text: Sad. I swear by the Quran, which is full of reminders of God, (that you are a Messenger).

1.author: Shakir
1.text: Suad, I swear by the Quran, full of admonition.

1.author: Wahiduddin Khan
1.text: Sad. By the Quran, full of admonition!

1.author: Yusuf Ali
1.text: Sad: By the Qur'an, Full of Admonition: (This is the Truth).

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: But the unbelievers are still full of pride and hostility.

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: In fact, the disbelievers are in false pride and opposition.

2.author: Arberry
2.text: nay, but the unbelievers glory in their schism.

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: Verily those who disbelieve are in vainglory and shism.

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: Nay, those who desbelieve are in false pride and opposition.

2.author: Itani
2.text: Those who disbelieve are steeped in arrogance and defiance.

2.author: Maududi
2.text: Nay, but the unbelievers are steeped in arrogance and stubborn defiance.

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: Those who disbelieve are in false pride and opposition.

2.author: Pickthall
2.text: Nay, but those who disbelieve are in false pride and schism.

2.author: Qarai
2.text: the faithless indeed dwell in conceit and defiance.

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: No, the unbelievers exalt in their division.

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: But those who disbelieve are in pride and dissension.

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: In fact, the unbelievers are the ones who are boastful and quarrelsome.

2.author: Shakir
2.text: Nay! those who disbelieve are in self-exaltation and opposition.

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: Those who deny the truth are steeped in arrogance and hostility.

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: But the Unbelievers (are steeped) in self-glory and Separatism.

2.author: Transliteration
2.text: Bali allatheena kafaroo fee AAizzatin washiqaqin

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: How many generations have We destroyed before them who cried (for mercy) when it was too late for escape.

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: Many a generation We did destroy before them – thereupon they cried out whereas it is not the time to escape!

3.author: Arberry
3.text: How many a generation We destroyed before them, and they called, but time was none to escape.

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: How many a generation have We destroyed afore them, and they cried when there was not time of fleeing.

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: How many a generation We have destroyed before them, and they cried out when there was no longer time for escape!

3.author: Itani
3.text: How many generations have We destroyed before them? They cried out when it was too late to escape.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: How many a nation did We destroy before them! (When they approached their doom) they cried out (for deliverance), but the time for deliverance was already past.

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: How many a generation have We destroyed before them! And they cried out when there was no longer time for escape.

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: How many a generation We destroyed before them, and they cried out when it was no longer the time for escape!

3.author: Qarai
3.text: How many a generation We have destroyed before them! They cried out [for help], but gone was the time for escape.

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: How many generations have We destroyed before them. They called: 'The time is neither of escape, nor safety'

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: How many a generation have We destroyed before them, and they [then] called out; but it was not a time for escape.

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: How many ancient generations did We destroy? (On facing Our torment) they cried out for help, but it was too late for them to escape.

3.author: Shakir
3.text: How many did We destroy before them of the generations, then they cried while the time of escaping had passed away.

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: How many generations We have destroyed before them! And they cried out when it was too late to escape.

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: How many generations before them did We destroy? In the end they cried (for mercy)- when there was no longer time for being saved!

3.author: Transliteration
3.text: Kam ahlakna min qablihim min qarnin fanadaw walata heena manasin

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: They were surprised that one of them had come to them as warner; and the unbelievers said: "He is a deceiving sorcerer.

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: And they were surprised that a Herald of Warning came to them from among themselves; and the disbelievers said, “He is a magician, a great liar!”

4.author: Arberry
4.text: Now they marvel that a warner has come to them from among them; and the unbelievers say, 'This is a lying sorcerer.

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: And they marvel that there should come Unto them a warner from among themselves. And the infidels say: this is a magician and a liar!

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: And they (Arab pagans) wonder that a warner (Prophet Muhammad SAW) has come to them from among themselves! And the disbelievers say: "This (Prophet Muhammad SAW) is a sorcerer, a liar.

4.author: Itani
4.text: And they marveled that a warner has come to them from among them. The disbelievers said, “This is a lying magician.”

4.author: Maududi
4.text: They wondered that a warner had come to them from among themselves, and the deniers of the Truth said: “This is a sorcerer, and a big liar.

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: And they wonder that a warner has come to them from among themselves. And the disbelievers say: "This is a sorcerer, a liar."

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: And they marvel that a warner from among themselves hath come unto them, and the disbelievers say: This is a wizard, a charlatan.

4.author: Qarai
4.text: They consider it odd that there should come to them a warner from among themselves, and the faithless say, ‘This is a magician, a mendacious liar.’

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: They marvel now that, from among themselves, a warner has come to them. The unbelievers say: 'This is a lying sorcerer.

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: And they wonder that there has come to them a warner from among themselves. And the disbelievers say, "This is a magician and a liar.

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: It seems strange to the pagans that a man from their own people should come to them as a Prophet. The unbelievers have said, "He is only a lying magician".

4.author: Shakir
4.text: And they wonder that there has come to them a warner from among themselves, and the disbelievers say: This is an enchanter, a liar.

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: They are surprised that a warner should come to them from among themselves. They say, "This is a magician, a great liar.

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: So they wonder that a Warner has come to them from among themselves! and the Unbelievers say, "This is a sorcerer telling lies!

4.author: Transliteration
4.text: WaAAajiboo an jaahum munthirun minhum waqala alkafiroona hatha sahirun kaththabun

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: Has he turned so many gods into one deity? This is indeed a strange thing!"

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: “Has he made all the Gods into One God? This is really something very strange!”

5.author: Arberry
5.text: What, has he made the gods One God? This is indeed a marvellous thing.'

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: Maketh he the gods One God? Verily that is a thing astounding!

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: "Has he made the aliha (gods) (all) into One Ilah (God - Allah). Verily, this is a curious thing!"

5.author: Itani
5.text: “Did he turn all the gods into one God? This is something strange.”

5.author: Maududi
5.text: Has he made the gods one single God? This is truly astounding.”

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: "Has he made the gods into One God. Verily, this is a curious thing!"

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: Maketh he the gods One Allah? Lo! that is an astounding thing.

5.author: Qarai
5.text: ‘Has he reduced the gods to one god? This is indeed an odd thing!’

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: What, has he made the gods One God? This is indeed a wondrous thing'

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: Has he made the gods [only] one God? Indeed, this is a curious thing."

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: They say, "Has he condemned all other gods but One? This is certainly strange".

5.author: Shakir
5.text: What! makes he the gods a single Allah? A strange thing is this, to be sure!

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: Does he make all the deities out to be one God? This is indeed a strange thing."

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: "Has he made the gods (all) into one Allah? Truly this is a wonderful thing!"

5.author: Transliteration
5.text: AjaAAala alalihata ilahan wahidan inna hatha lashayon AAujabun

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: And their leading chiefs said: "Remain attached to your gods. There is surely some motive behind it.

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: And their leaders went about, “Leave him and cling steadfastly to your Gods! Indeed he has a hidden objective in this!”

6.author: Arberry
6.text: And the Council of them depart, saying 'Go! Be steadfast to your gods; this is a thing to be desired.

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: The chiefs among them departed saying: go, and persevere in your gods; verily this is a thing designed.

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: And the leaders among them went about (saying): "Go on, and remain constant to your aliha (gods)! Verily, This is a thing designed (against you)!

6.author: Itani
6.text: The notables among them announced: “Go on, and hold fast to your gods. This is something planned.

6.author: Maududi
6.text: And the elders among them went forth saying: “Go ahead and be steadfast in adhering to your deities. What is being said is with a design.”

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: And the leaders among them went about (saying): "Go on, and remain constant to your gods! Verily, this is a thing designed!"

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: The chiefs among them go about, exhorting: Go and be staunch to your gods! Lo! this is a thing designed.

6.author: Qarai
6.text: Their elite go about [urging others]: ‘Go and stand by your gods! This is indeed the desirable thing [to do].

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: Their assembly left (saying): 'Go, and be patient to your gods, this is something to be desired.

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: And the eminent among them went forth, [saying], "Continue, and be patient over [the defense of] your gods. Indeed, this is a thing intended.

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: A group of the pagans walked out of a meeting with the Prophet and told the others, "Let us walk away. Be steadfast in the worship of your gods. This man wants to dominate you.

6.author: Shakir
6.text: And the chief persons of them break forth, saying: Go and steadily adhere to your gods; this is most surely a thing sought after.

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: Their leaders departed, saying, "Walk away! Hold fast to your deities. This is clearly a conspiracy.

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: And the leader among them go away (impatiently), (saying), "Walk ye away, and remain constant to your gods! For this is truly a thing designed (against you)!

6.author: Transliteration
6.text: Waintalaqa almalao minhum ani imshoo waisbiroo AAala alihatikum inna hatha lashayon yuradu

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: We never heard of it in the former faith. It is surely a fabrication.

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: “We never heard of this even in Christianity, the latest religion; this is clearly a newly fabricated matter.”

7.author: Arberry
7.text: We have not heard of this in the last religion; this is surely an invention.

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: We heard not thereof in the later faith; this is naught but an invention.

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: "We have not heard (the like) of this among the people of these later days. This is nothing but an invention!

7.author: Itani
7.text: We never heard of this in the former faith. This is nothing but a fabrication.

7.author: Maududi
7.text: We have not heard this in the religious community close to our time. This is nothing but a fabrication.

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: "We have not heard (the like) of this in the religion of these later days. This is nothing but an invention!"

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: We have not heard of this in later religion. This is naught but an invention.

7.author: Qarai
7.text: We did not hear of this in the latter-day creed. This is nothing but a fabrication.

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: We never heard of this in the former religion. It is nothing but an invention.

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: We have not heard of this in the latest religion. This is not but a fabrication.

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: We have heard nothing like this in the latest religion. This is only his own false invention.

7.author: Shakir
7.text: We never heard of this in the former faith; this is nothing but a forgery:

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: We have not heard of any such thing in the old religion. This is nothing but a fabrication.

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: "We never heard (the like) of this among the people of these latter days: this is nothing but a made-up tale!"

7.author: Transliteration
7.text: Ma samiAAna bihatha fee almillati alakhirati in hatha illa ikhtilaqun

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: To him of all of us has the Reminder been sent down?" They are still in doubt about My admonition; but they have not tasted My punishment yet!

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: “Is the Qur’an which is sent to him, among us?” In fact they are in a doubt concerning My Book; in fact, they have not yet tasted My punishment.

8.author: Arberry
8.text: What, has the Remembrance been sent down on him out of us all?' Nay, but they are in doubt of My Remembrance; nay, they have not yet tasted My chastisement.

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: Hath Unto him the admonition been sent down from amongst us! Yea! they are in doubt concerning My admonition. Yea! they have not yet tasted My torment.

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: "Has the Reminder been sent down to him (alone) from among us?" Nay! but they are in doubt about My Reminder (this Quran)! Nay, but they have not tasted (My) Torment!

8.author: Itani
8.text: Was the message sent down to him, out of all of us?” In fact, they are doubtful of My warning. In fact, they have not yet tasted My punishment.

8.author: Maududi
8.text: Has this Exhortation been sent down among us only to him, to the exclusion of all others?” Nay, they are in doubt regarding My Exhortation, and are saying all this because they have not yet had any taste of My chastisement.

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: "Has the Reminder been sent down to him (alone) from among us" Nay, but they are in doubt about My Reminder! Nay, but they have not tasted (My) torment!

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: Hath the reminder been unto him (alone) among us? Nay, but they are in doubt concerning My reminder; nay but they have not yet tasted My doom.

8.author: Qarai
8.text: Has the Reminder been sent down to him out of [all of] us?’ Indeed, they are in doubt concerning My Reminder. Indeed, they have not yet tasted My punishment.

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: What, out of all of us, has the Remembrance been sent down to him (Prophet Muhammad)' No, they are doubtful about My Remembrance, no, they have not yet tasted My punishment.

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: Has the message been revealed to him out of [all of] us?" Rather, they are in doubt about My message. Rather, they have not yet tasted My punishment.

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: Can it be that he alone has received the Quran?" In fact, they have doubts about My Quran and this is because they have not yet faced (My) torment.

8.author: Shakir
8.text: Has the reminder been revealed to him from among us? Nay! they are in doubt as to My reminder. Nay! they have not yet tasted My chastisement!

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: Was the message sent only to him out of all of us?" In fact, they doubt My warning; in fact, they have not yet tasted My punishment.

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: "What! has the Message been sent to him - (Of all persons) among us?"... but they are in doubt concerning My (Own) Message! Nay, they have not yet tasted My Punishment!

8.author: Transliteration
8.text: Aonzila AAalayhi alththikru min baynina bal hum fee shakkin min thikree bal lamma yathooqoo AAathabi

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: Do they have the stores of the mercy of your Lord, the mighty and munificent?

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: Or do they hold the treasures of the mercy of your Lord, the Almighty, the Great Bestower?

9.author: Arberry
9.text: Or have they the treasuries of thy Lord's mercy, the All-mighty, the All-giving?

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: Or, are with them the treasures of the mercy of thy Lord, the Bestower?

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: Or have they the treasures of the Mercy of your Lord, the All-Mighty, the Real Bestower?

9.author: Itani
9.text: Or do they possess the treasuries of the mercy of your Lord—the Majestic, the Giver?

9.author: Maududi
9.text: Do they possess the treasures of your Lord, the Most Mighty, the Great Bestower?

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: Or have they the treasures of the mercy of your Lord, the Almighty, the Real Bestower

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: Or are theirs the treasures of the mercy of thy Lord, the Mighty, the Bestower?

9.author: Qarai
9.text: Do they possess the treasuries of the mercy of your Lord, the All-mighty, the All-munificent?

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: Or, have they the treasuries of the Mercy of your Lord, the Almighty, the Giving?

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: Or do they have the depositories of the mercy of your Lord, the Exalted in Might, the Bestower?

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: Do they possess the treasures of the mercy of your, (Muhammad's), Lord, the Majestic and Munificent God?

9.author: Shakir
9.text: Or is it that they have the treasures of the mercy of your Lord, the Mighty, the great Giver?

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: Do they possess the treasures of the mercy of your Lord, the Mighty, the Great Bestower?

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: Or have they the treasures of the mercy of thy Lord,- the Exalted in Power, the Grantor of Bounties without measure?

9.author: Transliteration
9.text: Am AAindahum khazainu rahmati rabbika alAAazeezi alwahhabi

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: Or is the kingdom of the heavens and the earth and all that lies between them, theirs? Then let them climb up the ladders (to the heavens).

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: Is the kingdom of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them, for them? So would they not just ascend using ropes?

10.author: Arberry
10.text: Or is theirs the kingdom of the heavens and earth and of what between them is? Why, then let them ascend the cords!

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: Or, is theirs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and that which is in-between the twain? If so, let them ascend by steps.

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: Or is it that the dominion of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them is theirs? If so, let them ascend up with means (to the heavens)!

10.author: Itani
10.text: Or do they possess the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth and what is between them? Then let them ascend the ropes.

10.author: Maududi
10.text: Or do they possess the dominion of the heavens and the earth and of all that is in between them? If so, let them ascend the heights of the realm of causation and see!

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: Or is it that the dominion of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them is theirs If so, let them ascend up with means!

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: Or is the kingdom of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them theirs? Then let them ascend by ropes!

10.author: Qarai
10.text: Do they own the kingdom of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them? [If so,] let them ascend [to the higher spheres] by the means [of ascension].

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: Or, is theirs the Kingdom of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them? Then let them ascend by (their) means!

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: Or is theirs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and what is between them? Then let them ascend through [any] ways of access.

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: Do they own the heavens and the earth and all that is between them? Let them try on their own to block (the ways of heavens so that Our revelations cannot come to you).

10.author: Shakir
10.text: Or is it that theirs is the kingdom of the heavens and the earth and what is between them? Then let them ascend by any

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: Have they control over heavens and earth and whatever [lies] between them? Then let them climb up to heaven by ropes:

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: Or have they the dominion of the heavens and the earth and all between? If so, let them mount up with the ropes and means (to reach that end)!

10.author: Transliteration
10.text: Am lahum mulku alssamawati waalardi wama baynahuma falyartaqoo fee alasbabi

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: They will be one more army vanquished among the many routed hordes.

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: This is just one of the disgraced armies, that will be routed there and then.

11.author: Arberry
11.text: A very host of parties is routed there!

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: Here there is a host of the confederates only to be defeated.

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: (As they denied Allah's Message) they will be a defeated host like the confederates of the old times (who were defeated).

11.author: Itani
11.text: An army of confederates with be defeated there.

11.author: Maududi
11.text: This is only a small army out of the several armies that will suffer defeat here.

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: They will be a defeated host like the Confederates of the old times.

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: A defeated host are (all) the factions that are there.

11.author: Qarai
11.text: [They are but] a host routed out there of the factions.

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: The army is defeated as (were) the confederates.

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: [They are but] soldiers [who will be] defeated there among the companies [of disbelievers].

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: They are only a small band among the defeated confederate tribes.

11.author: Shakir
11.text: A host of deserters of the allies shall be here put to flight.

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: this host too, among other hosts, is bound to suffer defeat.

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: But there - will be put to flight even a host of confederates.

11.author: Transliteration
11.text: Jundun ma hunalika mahzoomun mina alahzabi

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: Even before them the people of Noah, 'Ad, the mighty Pharoah,

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: Before them, the people of Nooh had denied, and the tribe of A’ad, and Firaun who used to crucify.

12.author: Arberry
12.text: Cried lies before them the people of Noah, and Ad, and Pharaoh, he of the tent-pegs,

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: Before them there have belied the people of Nuh and the 'Aad, and Fir'awn the owner of the stakes.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: Before them (were many who) belied Messengers, the people of Nuh (Noah); and 'Ad; and Fir'aun (Pharaoh) the man of stakes (with which he used to punish the people),

12.author: Itani
12.text: Before them the people of Noah denied the truth; as did Aad, and Pharaoh of the Stakes.

12.author: Maududi
12.text: Before them the people of Noah, Ad, and Pharaoh of the tent-pegs gave the lie (to the Messengers)

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: Before them denied -- the people of Nuh; and `Ad; and Fir`awn the man of stakes,

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: The folk of Noah before them denied (their messenger) and (so did the tribe of) A'ad, and Pharaoh firmly planted,

12.author: Qarai
12.text: Before them Noah’s people impugned [their apostle] and [so did the people of] ‘Ad, and Pharaoh, the Impaler [of his victims],

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: Before them the nations of Noah, Aad and Pharaoh, and he of the tentpegs belied,

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: The people of Noah denied before them, and [the tribe of] 'Aad and Pharaoh, the owner of stakes,

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: The people of Noah, Ad and the dominating Pharaoh had rejected Our revelations.

12.author: Shakir
12.text: The people of Nuh and Ad, and Firon, the lord of spikes, rejected (apostles) before them.

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: Before them the people of Noah denied the truth, as did the 'Ad and Pharaoh of the Stakes,

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: Before them (were many who) rejected messengers,- the people of Noah, and 'Ad, and Pharaoh, the Lord of Stakes,

12.author: Transliteration
12.text: Kaththabat qablahum qawmu noohin waAAadun wafirAAawnu thoo alawtadi

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: And the Thamud, the people of Lot, as well as the dwellers of the Wood, had denied. These were the hordes.

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: And the tribe of Thamud, and the people of Lut, and the People of the Woods; these are the groups.

13.author: Arberry
13.text: and Thamood, and the people of Lot, and the men of the Thicket - those were the parties;

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: And the Thamud, and the people of Lut, and the dwellers of the wood; these were the confederates.

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: And Thamud, and the people of Lout (Lot), and the dwellers of the wood; such were the confederates.

13.author: Itani
13.text: And Thamood, and the people of Lot, and the dwellers of the Woods—these were the confederates.

13.author: Maududi
13.text: and so did Thamud and the people of Lot and the people of Aykah. These were all leagued together.

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: And Thamud, and the people of Lut, and the Dwellers of Al-Aykah; such were the Confederates.

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: And (the tribe of) Thamud, and the folk of Lot, and the dwellers in the wood: these were the factions.

13.author: Qarai
13.text: and Thamud, and the people of Lot, and the inhabitants of Aykah: those were the factions.

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: Thamood, the nation of Lot and the dwellers of the Thicket such were the confederates.

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: And [the tribe of] Thamud and the people of Lot and the companions of the thicket. Those are the companies.

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: So also did the people of Thamud, Lot, and the dwellers of the Forest.

13.author: Shakir
13.text: And Samood and the people of Lut and the dwellers of the thicket; these were the parties.

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: and the tribe of Thamud, and the people of Lot, and the dwellers of the Wood -- these were the confederates.

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: And Thamud, and the people of Lut, and the Companions of the Wood; - such were the Confederates.

13.author: Transliteration
13.text: Wathamoodu waqawmu lootin waashabu alaykati olaika alahzabu

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: Of all these there was not one who did not deny the messengers. So My retribution was justified.

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: None of them was such that it did not deny the Noble Messengers, therefore My punishment became inevitable.

14.author: Arberry
14.text: not one, that cried not lies to the Messengers, so My retribution was just.

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: There was not one but belied the apostles, wherefore justified was My wrath.

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: Not one of them but belied the Messengers, therefore My Torment was justified,

14.author: Itani
14.text: None of them but denied the messengers, so My retribution was deserved.

14.author: Maududi
14.text: Each of them gave the lie to Messengers and My decree of chastisement came upon them.

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: Not one of them but denied the Messengers; therefore My torment was justified.

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: Not one of them but did deny the messengers, therefor My doom was justified,

14.author: Qarai
14.text: There was not any one but such as impugned the apostles; so My retribution became due [against them].

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: There was not one of those that did not belie the Messengers. Therefore, My retribution was realized.

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: Each of them denied the messengers, so My penalty was justified.

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: Each of them who rejected the Messenger become subject to Our punishment.

14.author: Shakir
14.text: There was none of them but called the apostles liars, so just was My retribution.

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: There was not one of them but treated their messengers as liars, so My punishment rightly overtook them:

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: Not one (of them) but rejected the messengers, but My punishment came justly and inevitably (on them).

14.author: Transliteration
14.text: In kullun illa kaththaba alrrusula fahaqqa AAiqabi

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: They await but a single blast which will not be repeated.

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: They await just one Scream, which no one can avert.

15.author: Arberry
15.text: These are only awaiting for a single Cry, to which there is no delay.

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: And these wait but for one shout, wherefrom there will be no deferment.

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: And these only wait for a single Saihah [shout (i.e. the blowing of the Trumpet by the angel Israfil Sarafil)] there will be no pause or ending thereto [till everything will perish except Allah (the only God full of Majesty, Bounty and Honour)].

15.author: Itani
15.text: These can expect only a single scream, from which there is no recovery.

15.author: Maududi
15.text: They are waiting for nothing except a single Cry, after which there will be no second Cry.

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: And these only wait for a single Sayhah there will be no pause or ending thereto.

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: These wait for but one Shout, there will be no second thereto.

15.author: Qarai
15.text: These [too] do not await but a single Cry, which will not grant [them] any respite.

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: These only wait for a single Shout for which there will be no delay.

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: And these [disbelievers] await not but one blast [of the Horn]; for it there will be no delay.

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: They had only to wait for the single inevitable blast.

15.author: Shakir
15.text: Nor do these await aught but a single cry, there being no delay in it.

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: they have only to wait for one single blast [of punishment]: it shall not be delayed by one whit.

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: These (today) only wait for a single mighty Blast, which (when it comes) will brook no delay.

15.author: Transliteration
15.text: Wama yanthuru haolai illa sayhatan wahidatan ma laha min fawaqin

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: Still they say: "O Lord, give us our share before the Day of Reckoning."

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: And they said, “Our Lord! Give us our share quickly, before the Day of Reckoning.”

16.author: Arberry
16.text: They say, 'Our Lord, hasten to us our share before the Day of Reckoning.'

16.author: Daryabadi
16.text: And they say: our Lord! hasten our portion unto us before the Day of Reckoning

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: They say: "Our Lord! Hasten to us Qittana (i.e. our Record of good and bad deeds so that we see it) before the Day of Reckoning!"

16.author: Itani
16.text: And they say, “Our Lord, hasten Your writ upon us, before the Day of Account.”

16.author: Maududi
16.text: They say: “Our Lord, hasten to us our share (of chastisement) before the Day of Reckoning.”

16.author: Mubarakpuri
16.text: They say: "Our Lord! Hasten to us Qittana before the Day of Reckoning!"

16.author: Pickthall
16.text: They say: Our Lord! Hasten on for us our fate before the Day of Reckoning.

16.author: Qarai
16.text: They say, ‘Our Lord! Hasten on for us our share before the Day of Reckoning.’

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: They say: 'Our Lord, hasten to us our share before the Day of Recompense'

16.author: Saheeh International
16.text: And they say, "Our Lord, hasten for us our share [of the punishment] before the Day of Account"

16.author: Sarwar
16.text: They scornfully said, "Lord, show us the record of our deeds before the day when everyone must present the account of their deeds.".

16.author: Shakir
16.text: And they say: O our Lord! hasten on to us our portion before the day of reckoning.

16.author: Wahiduddin Khan
16.text: They say: "Our Lord! Hasten on for us our fate before the Day of Reckoning."

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: They say: "Our Lord! hasten to us our sentence (even) before the Day of Account!"

16.author: Transliteration
16.text: Waqaloo rabbana AAajjil lana qittana qabla yawmi alhisabi

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: Bear with patience what they say, and remember Our votary David, man of strength. He surely turned to Us in penitence.

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: Have patience upon what they say, and remember Our bondman Dawud, the one blessed with favours; he is indeed most inclined (towards His Lord).

17.author: Arberry
17.text: Bear patiently what they say, and remember Our servant David, the man of might; he was a penitent.

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: Bear thou with that which they say, and remember Our bondman, Da-ud, endured with strength; verily he was oft-returning onto Us.

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: Be patient (O Muhammad SAW) of what they say, and remember Our slave Dawud (David), endued with power. Verily, he was ever oft-returning in all matters and in repentance (toward Allah).

17.author: Itani
17.text: Be patient in the face of what they say, and mention Our servant David, the resourceful. He was obedient.

17.author: Maududi
17.text: (O Prophet), bear with patience what they say, and call to mind Our servant David, who was endowed with great strength and who constantly turned (to Allah).

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: Be patient of what they say and remember Our slave Dawud, endued with Al-Ayd. Verily, he was ever oft-returning in all matters and in repentance.

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: Bear with what they say, and remember Our bondman David, lord of might, Lo! he was ever turning in repentance (toward Allah).

17.author: Qarai
17.text: Be patient over what they say, and remember Our servant, David, [the man] of strength. Indeed he was a penitent [soul].

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: Bear patiently with what they say, and remember Our worshiper David, a man of might. He was ever turning in repentance.

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: Be patient over what they say and remember Our servant, David, the possessor of strength; indeed, he was one who repeatedly turned back [to Allah].

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: (Muhammad), bear patiently what they say and recall Our servant, David, who had strong hands and who was most repentant.

17.author: Shakir
17.text: Bear patiently what they say, and remember Our servant Dawood, the possessor of power; surely he was frequent in returning (to Allah).

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: Bear with their words patiently. Remember Our servant David, a man of strength who always turned to Us:

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: Have patience at what they say, and remember our servant David, the man of strength: for he ever turned (to Allah).

17.author: Transliteration
17.text: Isbir AAala ma yaqooloona waothkur AAabdana dawooda tha alaydi innahu awwabun

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: We subjugated the chiefs (of tribes) to struggle day and night with him,

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: Indeed We subjected the hills to say the praise with him, at night and at morn.

18.author: Arberry
18.text: With him We subjected the mountains to give glory at evening and sunrise,

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: Verily We so subjected the mountains that they should hallow Us with him at nightfall and sunrise.

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: Verily, We made the mountains to glorify Our Praises with him [Dawud (David)] in the 'Ashi (i.e. after the mid-day till sunset) and Ishraq (i.e. after the sunrise till mid-day).

18.author: Itani
18.text: We committed the mountains to glorify with him, in the evening and at daybreak.

18.author: Maududi
18.text: With him We had subjected the mountains that they join him in celebrating Allah's glory, evening and morning,

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: Verily, We made the mountains to glorify Our praises with him in the `Ashi and Ishraq.

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: Lo! We subdued the hills to hymn the praises (of their Lord) with him at nightfall and sunrise,

18.author: Qarai
18.text: We disposed the mountains to glorify [Allah] with him at evening and dawn,

18.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
18.text: We subjected the mountains to exalt (Me) with him in the evening and at sunrise,

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: Indeed, We subjected the mountains [to praise] with him, exalting [Allah] in the [late] afternoon and [after] sunrise.

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: We made the mountains join him in glorifying Us in the evening and in the morning.

18.author: Shakir
18.text: Surely We made the mountains to sing the glory (of Allah) in unison with him at the evening and the sunrise,

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: We made the mountains join him in glorifying Us at sunset and sunrise;

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: It was We that made the hills declare, in unison with him, Our Praises, at eventide and at break of day,

18.author: Transliteration
18.text: Inna sakhkharna aljibala maAAahu yusabbihna bialAAashiyyi waalishraqi

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: And the levied Tair. They were all obedient to him.

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: And birds gathered together; they were all obedient to him.

19.author: Arberry
19.text: and the birds, duly mustered, every one to him reverting;

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: And the birds also, gathering all oft-returning onto Him on his account.

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: And (so did) the birds assembled: all with him [Dawud (David)] did turn (to Allah i.e. glorified His Praises).

19.author: Itani
19.text: And the birds, gathered together. All obedient to him.

19.author: Maududi
19.text: and the birds, too, in their flocks, and turn again and again to celebrating Allah's glory.

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: And (so did) the birds assembled, all obedient to him.

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: And the birds assembled; all were turning unto Him.

19.author: Qarai
19.text: and the birds [as well], mustered [in flocks]; all echoing him [in a chorus].

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: and the birds, too, gathered each obedient to him.

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: And the birds were assembled, all with him repeating [praises].

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: We made the birds assemble around him in flocks.

19.author: Shakir
19.text: And the birds gathered together; all joined in singing with him.

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: and the birds, too, in flocks, all turned to Him.

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: And the birds gathered (in assemblies): all with him did turn (to Allah).

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: So We further strengthened his kingdom, and bestowed wisdom on him, and judgement in legal matters.

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: And We strengthened his kingdom and gave him wisdom and just speech.

20.author: Arberry
20.text: We strengthened his kingdom, and gave him wisdom and speech decisive.

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: And We made his dominion strong and vouchsafed him wisdom and decisive speech.

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: We made his kingdom strong and gave him Al-Hikmah (Prophethood, etc.) and sound judgement in speech and decision.

20.author: Itani
20.text: And We strengthened his kingdom, and gave him wisdom and decisive speech.

20.author: Maududi
20.text: And We strengthened his kingdom and endowed him with wisdom and decisive judgement.

20.author: Mubarakpuri
20.text: We made his kingdom strong and gave him Al-Hikmah and sound judgement in speech and decision.

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: We made his kingdom strong and gave him wisdom and decisive speech.

20.author: Qarai
20.text: We consolidated his kingdom and gave him wisdom and conclusive speech.

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: We made his kingdom strong and gave him wisdom and decisive speech.

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: And We strengthened his kingdom and gave him wisdom and discernment in speech.

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: We strengthened his kingdom, giving him wisdom and the power of sound Judgment.

20.author: Shakir
20.text: And We strengthened his kingdom and We gave him wisdom and a clear judgment.

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: We made his kingdom strong, and bestowed upon him wisdom and sagacity in judgement.

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: We strengthened his kingdom, and gave him wisdom and sound judgment in speech and decision.

20.author: Transliteration
20.text: Washadadna mulkahu waataynahu alhikmata wafasla alkhitabi

21.author: Ahmed Ali
21.text: Have you heard of the litigants who jumped over the wall into his chamber?

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: And did the news of the two disputants reach you? When they scaled over the wall into Dawud’s mosque.

21.author: Arberry
21.text: Has the tiding of the dispute come to thee? When they scaled the Sanctuary,

21.author: Daryabadi
21.text: And hath the tidings of the contending parties reached thee, when they walled the apartment?

21.author: Hilali & Khan
21.text: And has the news of the litigants reached you? When they climbed over the wall into (his) Mihrab (a praying place or a private room,).

21.author: Itani
21.text: Has the story of the two disputants reached you? When they scaled the sanctuary?

21.author: Maududi
21.text: Has the story of the litigants reached you -- of those who entered his private chambers by climbing over the wall?

21.author: Mubarakpuri
21.text: And has the news of the litigants reached you When they climbed over the wall into (his) Mihrab (private chamber of worship);

21.author: Pickthall
21.text: And hath the story of the litigants come unto thee? How they climbed the wall into the royal chamber;

21.author: Qarai
21.text: Has there not come to you the account of the contenders, when they scaled the wall into the sanctuary?

21.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
21.text: Has the news of the dispute reached you (Prophet Muhammad)? When they scaled the Sanctuary

21.author: Saheeh International
21.text: And has there come to you the news of the adversaries, when they climbed over the wall of [his] prayer chamber -

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: Have you heard the news of the disputing parties who climbed the walls of the prayer room

21.author: Shakir
21.text: And has there come to you the story of the litigants, when they made an entry into the private chamber by ascending over the walls?

21.author: Wahiduddin Khan
21.text: Have you heard the story of the disputants who entered his chamber by climbing over the wall?

21.author: Yusuf Ali
21.text: Has the Story of the Disputants reached thee? Behold, they climbed over the wall of the private chamber;

21.author: Transliteration
21.text: Wahal ataka nabao alkhasmi ith tasawwaroo almihraba

22.author: Ahmed Ali
22.text: When they came before David, he was frightened of them. "Do not be afraid," they said. "The two of us are disputing the wrong one has done the other. So judge between us with equity, and do not be unjust, and guide us to the right path.

22.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
22.text: When they entered upon David, so he feared them – they said, “Do not fear! We are two disputants, one of whom has wronged the other, therefore judge fairly between us and do not judge unjustly – and show us the right way.”

22.author: Arberry
22.text: when they entered upon David, and he took fright at them; and they said, 'Fear not; two disputants we are -- one of us has injured the other; so judge between us justly, and transgress not, and guide us to the right path.'

22.author: Daryabadi
22.text: When they went in unto Da-ud, he was frightened at them. They said: fear not we are two contending parties; one of us hath oppressed the other, so judge between us with truth, and be not iniquitious, and guide us unto the even path.

22.author: Hilali & Khan
22.text: When they entered in upon Dawud (David), he was terrified of them, they said: "Fear not! (We are) two litigants, one of whom has wronged the other, therefore judge between us with truth, and treat us not with injustice, and guide us to the Right Way.

22.author: Itani
22.text: When they entered upon David, and he was startled by them. They said, “Do not fear. Two disputants; one of us has wronged the other; so judge between us fairly, and do not be biased, and guide us to the straight way.”

22.author: Maududi
22.text: As they came upon David -- and he was frightened of them -- they said: “Be not afraid. We are just two litigants: one of us has committed excess against the other. So judge rightly between us, and be not unjust; and guide us to the Right Way.

22.author: Mubarakpuri
22.text: When they entered in upon Dawud, he was terrified of them. They said: "Fear not! (We are) two litigants, one of us has wronged the other, therefore judge between us with truth, and treat us not with injustice, and guide us to the right way.

22.author: Pickthall
22.text: How they burst in upon David, and he was afraid of them. They said: Be not afraid! (We are) two litigants, one of whom hath wronged the other, therefor judge aright between us; be not unjust; and show us the fair way.

22.author: Qarai
22.text: When they entered into the presence of David, he was alarmed by them. They said, ‘Do not be afraid. [We are only] two contenders: one of us has bullied the other. So judge justly between us, and do not exceed [the bounds of justice], and show us the right path.’

22.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
22.text: they went to David whereupon he was afraid of them, but they said: 'Do not be afraid, we are two that have a dispute, one of us has wronged the other. Judge between us justly, and do not transgress, and guide us to the Right Path.

22.author: Saheeh International
22.text: When they entered upon David and he was alarmed by them? They said, "Fear not. [We are] two adversaries, one of whom has wronged the other, so judge between us with truth and do not exceed [it] and guide us to the sound path.

22.author: Sarwar
22.text: and entered where David was (praying). He was frightened, so they said, "Do not be afraid. We are only two disputing parties of which one of us has transgressed against the other. Judge between us with truth and justice and guide us to the right path".

22.author: Shakir
22.text: When they entered in upon Dawood and he was frightened at them, they said: Fear not; two litigants, of whom one has acted wrongfully towards the other, therefore decide between us with justice, and do not act unjustly, and guide us to the right way.

22.author: Wahiduddin Khan
22.text: When they reached David, he took fright, but they said, "Do not be afraid. We are two disputants, one of whom has wronged the other: judge between us fairly, do not be unjust, and guide us to the right path.

22.author: Yusuf Ali
22.text: When they entered the presence of David, and he was terrified of them, they said: "Fear not: we are two disputants, one of whom has wronged the other: Decide now between us with truth, and treat us not with injustice, but guide us to the even Path..

22.author: Transliteration
22.text: Ith dakhaloo AAala dawooda fafaziAAa minhum qaloo la takhaf khasmani bagha baAAduna AAala baAAdin faohkum baynana bialhaqqi wala tushtit waihdina ila sawai alssirati

23.author: Ahmed Ali
23.text: This man here is my brother. He has ninety and nine ewes while I have only one. He demands that I should give him my ewe, and wants to get the better of me in argument."

23.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
23.text: “This is my brother; he has ninety nine ewes and I have one ewe; and he now says ‘Give that one also to me’ – and he is very demanding in speech.”

23.author: Arberry
23.text: 'Behold, this my brother has ninety-nine ewes, and I have one ewe. So he said, "Give her into my charge"; and he overcame me in the argument.'

23.author: Daryabadi
23.text: Verily this my brother hath nine and ninety ewes while I have one ewe; and he saith: entrust it to me, and he hath prevailed upon me in speech.

23.author: Hilali & Khan
23.text: Verily, this my brother (in religion) has ninety nine ewes, while I have (only) one ewe, and he says: "Hand it over to me, and he overpowered me in speech."

23.author: Itani
23.text: “This brother of mine has ninety nine ewes, and I have one ewe, and he said, ‘Entrust it to me,’ and he pressured me with words.”

23.author: Maududi
23.text: Behold, this is my brother; he has ninety-nine ewes and I have only one ewe.” And yet he said: “Give her into my charge,” and he got the better of me in argument.

23.author: Mubarakpuri
23.text: Verily, this my brother (in religion) has ninety-nine ewes, while I have (only) one ewe, and he says: "Hand it over to me, and he overpowered me in speech."

23.author: Pickthall
23.text: Lo! this my brother hath ninety and nine ewes while I had one ewe; and he said: Entrust it to me, and he conquered me in speech.

23.author: Qarai
23.text: ‘This brother of mine has ninety-nine ewes, while I have only a single ewe, and [yet] he says, ‘Commit it to my care,’ and he browbeats me in speech.’

23.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
23.text: This, my brother has ninetynine ewes, but I have only one ewe (a female sheep). He said: "Give her into my keeping" and overcame me in the argument'

23.author: Saheeh International
23.text: Indeed this, my brother, has ninety-nine ewes, and I have one ewe; so he said, 'Entrust her to me,' and he overpowered me in speech."

23.author: Sarwar
23.text: One of them said, "This is my brother who has ninety-nine ewes when I have only one. He has demanded me to place that one in his custody; he had the stronger argument".

23.author: Shakir
23.text: Surely this is my brother; he has ninety-nine ewes and I have a single ewe; but he said: Make it over to me, and he has prevailed against me in discourse.

23.author: Wahiduddin Khan
23.text: "This brother of mine has ninety-nine ewes and I have only one. He said, "Let me have charge of it," and got the better of me with his words."

23.author: Yusuf Ali
23.text: "This man is my brother: He has nine and ninety ewes, and I have (but) one: Yet he says, 'commit her to my care,' and is (moreover) harsh to me in speech."

23.author: Transliteration
23.text: Inna hatha akhee lahu tisAAun watisAAoona naAAjatan waliya naAAjatun wahidatun faqala akfilneeha waAAazzanee fee alkhitabi

24.author: Ahmed Ali
24.text: (David) said: "He is unjust in demanding your ewe to add to his (many) ewes. Many partners are surely -- unjust to one another, except those who believe and do the right; but there are only a few of them." It occurred to David that he was being tried by Us, and he begged his Lord to forgive him, and fell down in homage and repented.

24.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
24.text: Said Dawud, “He is indeed being unjust to you in that he demands to add your ewe to his ewes; and indeed most partners wrong one another, except those who believe and do good deeds – and they are very few!” Thereupon Dawud realised that We had tested him, so he sought forgiveness from his Lord, and fell prostrate and inclined (towards his Lord). (Command of Prostration # 10)

24.author: Arberry
24.text: Said he, 'Assuredly he has wronged thee in asking for thy ewe in addition to his sheep; and indeed many intermixers do injury one against the other, save those who believe, and do deeds of righteousness -- and how few they are!' And David thought that We had only tried him; therefore he sought forgiveness of his Lord, and he fell down, bowing, and he repented.

24.author: Daryabadi
24.text: Da-ud said: assuredly he hath wronged thee in demanding thine ewe in addition to his ewes, and verily many of the partners oppress each other save such as believe and work righteous works, and few are they. And Da-ud imagined that We had tried him; so he asked forgiveness of his Lord, and he fell down bowing and turned in penitence.

24.author: Hilali & Khan
24.text: [Dawud (David)] said (immediately without listening to the opponent): "He has wronged you in demanding your ewe in addition to his ewes. And, verily, many partners oppress one another, except those who believe and do righteous good deeds, and they are few." And Dawud (David) guessed that We have tried him and he sought Forgiveness of his Lord, and he fell down prostrate and turned (to Allah) in repentance.

24.author: Itani
24.text: He said, “He has done you wrong by asking your ewe in addition to his ewes. Many partners take advantage of one another, except those who believe and do good deeds, but these are so few.” David realized that We were testing him, so he sought forgiveness from his Lord, and fell down to his knees, and repented.

24.author: Maududi
24.text: David said: “He has certainly wronged you in seeking to add your ewe to his ewes; and indeed many who live together commit excesses, one to the other, except those that believe and act righteously; and they are but few.” (While so saying) David realized that it is We Who have put him to test; therefore, he sought the forgiveness of his Lord, and fell down, bowing and penitently turning (to Him).

24.author: Mubarakpuri
24.text: [Dawud] said: "He has wronged you in demanding your ewe in addition to his ewes. And, verily, many partners oppress one another, except those who believe and do righteous good deeds, and they are few." And Dawud guessed that We have tried him and he sought forgiveness of his Lord, and he fell down prostrate and turned (to Allah) in repentance.

24.author: Pickthall
24.text: (David) said: He hath wronged thee in demanding thine ewe in addition to his ewes, and lo! many partners oppress one another, save such as believe and do good works, and they are few. And David guessed that We had tried him, and he sought forgiveness of his Lord, and he bowed himself and fell down prostrate and repented.

24.author: Qarai
24.text: He said, ‘He has certainly wronged you by asking your ewe in addition to his own ewes, and indeed many partners bully one another, except such as have faith and do righteous deeds, and few are they.’ Then David knew that We had tested him, whereat he pleaded with his Lord for forgiveness, and fell down in prostration and repented.

24.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
24.text: He (David) replied: 'He has without doubt wronged you in seeking to add your ewe to his sheep. Many intermixers wrong one another; except those who believe, and do good works, and they are few indeed' David realized that We had tried him and sought the forgiveness of his Lord and fell down, prostrate and repented.

24.author: Saheeh International
24.text: [David] said, "He has certainly wronged you in demanding your ewe [in addition] to his ewes. And indeed, many associates oppress one another, except for those who believe and do righteous deeds - and few are they." And David became certain that We had tried him, and he asked forgiveness of his Lord and fell down bowing [in prostration] and turned in repentance [to Allah].

24.author: Sarwar
24.text: David said, "He has certainly been unjust in demanding your ewe from you. Most partners transgress against each other except for the righteously striving believers who are very few." David realized that it was a test from Us so he asked forgiveness from his Lord and knelt down before him in repentance.

24.author: Shakir
24.text: He said: Surely he has been unjust to you in demanding your ewe (to add) to his own ewes; and most surely most of the partners act wrongfully towards one another, save those who believe and do good, and very few are they; and Dawood was sure that We had tried him, so he sought the protection of his Lord and he fell down bowing and turned time after time (to Him).

24.author: Wahiduddin Khan
24.text: David said, "He has certainly wronged you by demanding that your ewe be added to his ewes! Thus many partners wrong one another -- [all] save those who believe [in God] and do righteous deeds: but how few are they!" And [suddenly] David understood that We were only putting him to the test, and so he asked his Lord to forgive him his sins; he fell to his knees, and turned to Him in repentance.

24.author: Yusuf Ali
24.text: (David) said: "He has undoubtedly wronged thee in demanding thy (single) ewe to be added to his (flock of) ewes: truly many are the partners (in business) who wrong each other: Not so do those who believe and work deeds of righteousness, and how few are they?"... and David gathered that We had tried him: he asked forgiveness of his Lord, fell down, bowing (in prostration), and turned (to Allah in repentance).

24.author: Transliteration
24.text: Qala laqad thalamaka bisuali naAAjatika ila niAAajihi wainna katheeran mina alkhulatai layabghee baAAduhum AAala baAAdin illa allatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati waqaleelun ma hum wathanna dawoodu annama fatannahu faistaghfara rabbahu wakharra rakiAAan waanaba

25.author: Ahmed Ali
25.text: So We forgave him. He has surely a high rank with Us and an excellent place of return.

25.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
25.text: We therefore forgave him this; and indeed for him in Our presence are, surely, proximity and an excellent abode.

25.author: Arberry
25.text: Accordingly We forgave him that, and he has a near place in Our presence and a fair resort.

25.author: Daryabadi
25.text: So We forgave him that; and verily for him is an approach with us, and a happy retreat.

25.author: Hilali & Khan
25.text: So We forgave him that, and verily, for him is a near access to Us, and a good place of (final) return (Paradise).

25.author: Itani
25.text: So We forgave him that. And for him is nearness to Us, and a good place of return.

25.author: Maududi
25.text: Thereupon We forgave him his shortcoming and indeed (an exalted position of) nearness awaits him, and an excellent resort.

25.author: Mubarakpuri
25.text: So, We forgave him that, and verily, for him is a near access to Us, and a good place of return.

25.author: Pickthall
25.text: So We forgave him that; and lo! he had access to Our presence and a happy journey's end.

25.author: Qarai
25.text: So We forgave him that, and indeed he has [a station of] nearness with Us and a good destination.

25.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
25.text: So, We forgave him that, and he has a near place with Us and a fine return.

25.author: Saheeh International
25.text: So We forgave him that; and indeed, for him is nearness to Us and a good place of return.

25.author: Sarwar
25.text: We forgave him for this. In Our eyes he certainly has a good position and the best share (of the world to come).

25.author: Shakir
25.text: Therefore We rectified for him this, and most surely he had a nearness to Us and an excellent resort.

25.author: Wahiduddin Khan
25.text: We forgave him his sins. His reward will be nearness to Us, a good place to return to.

25.author: Yusuf Ali
25.text: So We forgave him this (lapse): he enjoyed, indeed, a Near Approach to Us, and a beautiful place of (Final) Return.

25.author: Transliteration
25.text: Faghafarna lahu thalika wainna lahu AAindana lazulfa wahusna maabin

26.author: Ahmed Ali
26.text: "O David, We have made you trustee on the earth. So judge between men equitably, and do not follow your lust lest it should lead you astray from the way of God. Surely for those who go astray from the way of God, is severe punishment, for having forgotten the Day of Reckoning.

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: “O Dawud! We have indeed appointed you as a Viceroy in the earth, therefore judge between mankind with the truth, and do not follow desire for it will lead you astray from Allah’s path; indeed for those who stray away from Allah’s path is a severe punishment, because they forgot the Day of Reckoning.”

26.author: Arberry
26.text: 'David, behold, We have appointed thee a viceroy in the earth; therefore judge between men justly, and follow not caprice, lest it lead thee astray from the way of God. Surely those who go astray from the way of God -- there awaits them a terrible chastisement, for that they have forgotten the Day of Reckoning.'

26.author: Daryabadi
26.text: O Da-ud! verily We have appointed thee a vicegerent in the earth, so judge between mankind with truth, and follow not desire, lest it cause thee to err from the path of Allah. Verily those who err from the path of Allah - Unto them shall be a severe torment, for they forgat the Day of Reckoning.

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: O Dawud (David)! Verily! We have placed you as a successor on earth, so judge you between men in truth (and justice) and follow not your desire for it will mislead you from the Path of Allah. Verily! Those who wander astray from the Path of Allah (shall) have a severe torment, because they forgot the Day of Reckoning.

26.author: Itani
26.text: “O David, We made you a ruler in the land, so judge between the people with justice, and do not follow desire, lest it diverts you from God’s path. Those who stray from God’s path will have a painful punishment, for having ignored the Day of Account.”

26.author: Maududi
26.text: (We said to him): “O David, We have appointed you vicegerent on earth. Therefore, rule among people with justice and do not follow (your) desire lest it should lead you astray from Allah's Path. Allah's severe chastisement awaits those who stray away from Allah's Path, for they had forgotten the Day of Reckoning.

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: O Dawud! Verily, We have placed you as a successor on the earth; so judge you between men in truth (and justice) and follow not your desire -- for it will mislead you from the path of Allah. Verily, those who wander astray from the path of Allah (shall) have a severe torment, because they forgot the Day of Reckoning.

26.author: Pickthall
26.text: (And it was said unto him): O David! Lo! We have set thee as a viceroy in the earth; therefor judge aright between mankind, and follow not desire that it beguile thee from the way of Allah. Lo! those who wander from the way of Allah have an awful doom, forasmuch as they forgot the Day of Reckoning.

26.author: Qarai
26.text: ‘O David! Indeed, We have made you a vicegerent on the earth. So judge between people with justice, and do not follow your desires, or they will lead you astray from the way of Allah. Indeed there is a severe punishment for those who stray from the way of Allah, because of their forgetting the Day of Reckoning.’

26.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
26.text: (We said): 'David, We have made you a caliph in the earth. Judge with justice among people and do not yield to your own preference in case it should lead you from the Path of Allah. Surely, a terrible punishment awaits those who stray from the Path of Allah, because they forget the Day of Reckoning'

26.author: Saheeh International
26.text: [We said], "O David, indeed We have made you a successor upon the earth, so judge between the people in truth and do not follow [your own] desire, as it will lead you astray from the way of Allah." Indeed, those who go astray from the way of Allah will have a severe punishment for having forgotten the Day of Account.

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: We told him. "David, We have appointed you as Our deputy on earth so judge among the people with truth. Do not follow (worldly) desires lest you go astray from the way of God. Those who go astray from the way of God will suffer severe torment for forgetting the Day of Reckoning.

26.author: Shakir
26.text: o Dawood! surely We have made you a ruler in the land; so judge between men with justice and do not follow desire, lest it should lead you astray from the path of Allah; (as for) those who go astray from the path of Allah, they shall surely have a severe punishment because they forgot the day of reckoning.

26.author: Wahiduddin Khan
26.text: We said, "David, We have given you mastery over the land. Judge fairly between people. Do not follow your desires, lest they divert you from God's path: those who wander from His path will have a severe punishment, because they ignore the Day of Reckoning."

26.author: Yusuf Ali
26.text: O David! We did indeed make thee a vicegerent on earth: so judge thou between men in truth (and justice): Nor follow thou the lusts (of thy heart), for they will mislead thee from the Path of Allah: for those who wander astray from the Path of Allah, is a Penalty Grievous, for that they forget the Day of Account.

26.author: Transliteration
26.text: Ya dawoodu inna jaAAalnaka khaleefatan fee alardi faohkum bayna alnnasi bialhaqqi wala tattabiAAi alhawa fayudillaka AAan sabeeli Allahi inna allatheena yadilloona AAan sabeeli Allahi lahum AAathabun shadeedun bima nasoo yawma alhisabi

27.author: Ahmed Ali
27.text: We have not created the heavens and the earth and all that lies between them, all for nothing. Only those who deny imagine so. So for the unbelievers there is woe from the fire.

27.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
27.text: And We have not created the heaven and the earth and all that is between them without purpose; this is what the disbelievers assume; therefore ruin is for the disbelievers, by the fire.

27.author: Arberry
27.text: We have not created the heavens and earth, and what between them is, for vanity; such is the thought of the unbelievers, wherefore woe unto the unbelievers because of the Fire!

27.author: Daryabadi
27.text: And We have not created the heaven and the earth and whatsoever is in-between the twain in vain." That is the opinion of those who disbelieve. And woe Unto those who disbelieve the Fire."

27.author: Hilali & Khan
27.text: And We created not the heaven and the earth and all that is between them without purpose! That is the consideration of those who disbelieve! Then woe to those who disbelieve (in Islamic Monotheism) from the Fire!

27.author: Itani
27.text: We did not create the heaven and the earth and everything between them in vain. That is the assumption of those who disbelieve—so woe to those who disbelieve because of the Fire.

27.author: Maududi
27.text: We did not create this heaven and earth and all that lies between them in vain. That is the fancy of those who denied the Truth. So woe from the Fire to all who deny the Truth.

27.author: Mubarakpuri
27.text: And We created not the heaven and the earth and all that is between them without purpose! That is the consideration of those who disbelieve! Then woe to those who disbelieve from the Fire!

27.author: Pickthall
27.text: And We created not the heaven and the earth and all that is between them in vain. That is the opinion of those who disbelieve. And woe unto those who disbelieve, from the Fire!

27.author: Qarai
27.text: We did not create the sky and the earth and whatever is between them in vain. That is a conjecture of the faithless. So woe to the faithless for the Fire!

27.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
27.text: It was not in falsehood that We created the heavens and the earth and all that is between them. That is the thought of the unbelievers. But woe to the unbelievers because of the Fire!

27.author: Saheeh International
27.text: And We did not create the heaven and the earth and that between them aimlessly. That is the assumption of those who disbelieve, so woe to those who disbelieve from the Fire.

27.author: Sarwar
27.text: We have not created the heavens and the earth and all that is between them without purpose, even though this is the belief of the disbelievers. Woe to the disbelievers; they will suffer the torment of fire

27.author: Shakir
27.text: And We did not create the heaven and the earth and what is between them in vain; that is the opinion of those who disbelieve then woe to those who disbelieve on account of the fire.

27.author: Wahiduddin Khan
27.text: We did not create heaven and earth and all that is between them in vain. That is the opinion of those who deny the truth. Woe betide those who deny the truth, when they are cast into the Fire --

27.author: Yusuf Ali
27.text: Not without purpose did We create heaven and earth and all between! that were the thought of Unbelievers! but woe to the Unbelievers because of the Fire (of Hell)!

27.author: Transliteration
27.text: Wama khalaqna alssamaa waalarda wama baynahuma batilan thalika thannu allatheena kafaroo fawaylun lillatheena kafaroo mina alnnari

28.author: Ahmed Ali
28.text: Should We equate those who do the right with those who spread corruption in the land? Should We make those who are morally integrated equal to those who seek disintegration?

28.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
28.text: Shall We make those who believe and do good deeds equal to those who spread turmoil in the earth? Or shall We equate the pious with the disobedient?

28.author: Arberry
28.text: Or shall We make those who believe and do righteous deeds as the workers of corruption in the earth, or shall We make the godfearing as the transgressors?

28.author: Daryabadi
28.text: Shall We make those who believe and work righteous works I i ke Unto the corrupters in the earth; Or Shall We make the God-fearing like Unto the wicked!

28.author: Hilali & Khan
28.text: Shall We treat those who believe (in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism) and do righteous good deeds, as Mufsidun (those who associate partners in worship with Allah and commit crimes) on earth? Or shall We treat the Muttaqun (pious - see V. 2:2), as the Fujjar (criminals, disbelievers, wicked, etc)?

28.author: Itani
28.text: Or are We to treat those who believe and do righteous deeds like those who make trouble on earth? Or are We to treat the pious like the shameless?

28.author: Maududi
28.text: Shall We then treat alike those that believe and act righteously and those that create mischief on earth? Or treat alike the God-fearing and the wicked?

28.author: Mubarakpuri
28.text: Shall We treat those who believe and do righteous good deeds as those who cause mischief on the earth Or shall We treat those who have Taqwa as the evildoers

28.author: Pickthall
28.text: Shall We treat those who believe and do good works as those who spread corruption in the earth; or shall We treat the pious as the wicked?

28.author: Qarai
28.text: Shall We treat those who have faith and do righteous deeds like those who cause corruption on the earth? Shall We treat the Godwary like the vicious?

28.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
28.text: Are We to make those who believe and do good works the same as those who corrupt the earth? Are We to make the righteous as the wicked?

28.author: Saheeh International
28.text: Or should we treat those who believe and do righteous deeds like corrupters in the land? Or should We treat those who fear Allah like the wicked?

28.author: Sarwar
28.text: Do We consider the righteously striving believers equal to the evil-doers in the land? Are the pious ones equal to those who openly commit sin?

28.author: Shakir
28.text: Shall We treat those who believe and do good like the mischief-makers in the earth? Or shall We make those who guard (against evil) like the wicked?

28.author: Wahiduddin Khan
28.text: shall We treat those who believe and do good works the same as those who spread corruption in the land; shall We treat the pious the same as the wicked?

28.author: Yusuf Ali
28.text: Shall We treat those who believe and work deeds of righteousness, the same as those who do mischief on earth? Shall We treat those who guard against evil, the same as those who turn aside from the right?

28.author: Transliteration
28.text: Am najAAalu allatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati kaalmufsideena fee alardi am najAAalu almuttaqeena kaalfujjari

29.author: Ahmed Ali
29.text: We have sent down a Book to you which is blessed, so that people may apply their minds to its revelations, and the men of wisdom may reflect.

29.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
29.text: This is a Book which We have sent down upon you, a blessed Book, for them to ponder upon its verses, and for men of intellect to accept advice.

29.author: Arberry
29.text: A Book We have sent down to thee, Blessed, that men possessed of minds may ponder its signs and so remember.

29.author: Daryabadi
29.text: This is a Book We have sent down Unto thee, blest, that they may ponder the revelations thereof, and that there may be admonished men of understanding.

29.author: Hilali & Khan
29.text: (This is) a Book (the Quran) which We have sent down to you, full of blessings that they may ponder over its Verses, and that men of understanding may remember.

29.author: Itani
29.text: A blessed Book that We sent down to you, that they may ponder its Verses, and for those with intelligence to take heed.

29.author: Maududi
29.text: This is the Blessed Book that We have revealed to you, (O Muhammad), that people with understanding may reflect over its verses and those with understanding derive a lesson.

29.author: Mubarakpuri
29.text: (This is) a Book which We have sent down to you, full of blessings, that they may ponder over its Verses, and that men of understanding may remember.

29.author: Pickthall
29.text: (This is) a Scripture that We have revealed unto thee, full of blessing, that they may ponder its revelations, and that men of understanding may reflect.

29.author: Qarai
29.text: [This is] a blessed Book that We have sent down to you, so that they may contemplate its signs, and that those who possess intellect may take admonition.

29.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
29.text: It is a Blessed Book that We have sent down to you (Prophet Muhammad), so that those possessed with minds might ponder its verses and remember.

29.author: Saheeh International
29.text: [This is] a blessed Book which We have revealed to you, [O Muhammad], that they might reflect upon its verses and that those of understanding would be reminded.

29.author: Sarwar
29.text: It is a blessed Book which We have revealed for you so that you will reflect upon its verses and so the people of understanding will take heed.

29.author: Shakir
29.text: (It is) a Book We have revealed to you abounding in good that they may ponder over its verses, and that those endowed with understanding may be mindful.

29.author: Wahiduddin Khan
29.text: This is a blessed Book which We sent down to you [Muhammad], for people to ponder over its messages, and for those with understanding to take heed.

29.author: Yusuf Ali
29.text: (Here is) a Book which We have sent down unto thee, full of blessings, that they may mediate on its Signs, and that men of understanding may receive admonition.

29.author: Transliteration
29.text: Kitabun anzalnahu ilayka mubarakun liyaddabbaroo ayatihi waliyatathakkara oloo alalbabi

30.author: Ahmed Ali
30.text: We bestowed Solomon on David who was an excellent devotee, for he turned to God in penitence.

30.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
30.text: And We bestowed Sulaiman to Dawud; what an excellent bondman! He is indeed most inclined.

30.author: Arberry
30.text: And We gave unto David Solomon; how excellent a servant he was! He was a penitent.

30.author: Daryabadi
30.text: And We vouchsafed Unto Da-ud Sulaiman. How excellent a bondman! j Verily he was oft-returning.'

30.author: Hilali & Khan
30.text: And to Dawud (David) We gave Sulaiman (Solomon). How excellent (a) slave! Verily, he was ever oft-returning in repentance (to Us)!

30.author: Itani
30.text: And We granted David, Solomon, an excellent servant. He was penitent.

30.author: Maududi
30.text: We bestowed upon David (an illustrious son), Solomon. How excellent a servant (of Ours he was)! Indeed he constantly turned to Us in devotion.

30.author: Mubarakpuri
30.text: And to Dawud We gave Sulayman. How excellent a servant! Verily, he was ever turning in repentance (to Us)!

30.author: Pickthall
30.text: And We bestowed on David, Solomon. How excellent a slave! Lo! he was ever turning in repentance (toward Allah).

30.author: Qarai
30.text: And to David We gave Solomon—what an excellent servant he was! Indeed, he was a penitent [soul].

30.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
30.text: We gave Solomon to David; and he was an excellent worshiper, he was penitent.

30.author: Saheeh International
30.text: And to David We gave Solomon. An excellent servant, indeed he was one repeatedly turning back [to Allah].

30.author: Sarwar
30.text: We granted Solomon to David, a blessed servant of Ours and certainly the most repentant person.

30.author: Shakir
30.text: And We gave to Dawood Sulaiman, most excellent the servant! Surely he was frequent in returning (to Allah).

30.author: Wahiduddin Khan
30.text: We gave David Solomon. He was an excellent servant who always turned to God.

30.author: Yusuf Ali
30.text: To David We gave Solomon (for a son),- How excellent in Our service! Ever did he turn (to Us)!

30.author: Transliteration
30.text: Wawahabna lidawooda sulaymana niAAma alAAabdu innahu awwabun

31.author: Ahmed Ali
31.text: When they brought fleet-footed chargers in the evening to show him,

31.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
31.text: When fast footed steeds were presented to him at evening.

31.author: Arberry
31.text: When in the evening were presented to him the standing steeds,

31.author: Daryabadi
31.text: Recall what time there were presented Unto him, at eventide, s coursers swift-footed.

31.author: Hilali & Khan
31.text: When there were displayed before him, in the afternoon, well trained horses of the highest breed [for Jihad (holy fighting in Allah's Cause)].

31.author: Itani
31.text: When the beautiful horses were paraded before him in the evening.

31.author: Maududi
31.text: And when one evening well-trained and running horses of noble breed were brought to him

31.author: Mubarakpuri
31.text: When there were displayed before him, in the afternoon, well trained horses of the highest breed.

31.author: Pickthall
31.text: When there were shown to him at eventide lightfooted coursers

31.author: Qarai
31.text: One evening when there were displayed before him prancing steeds,

31.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
