31.text: When his dressage steeds were presented to him in the evening,

31.author: Saheeh International
31.text: [Mention] when there were exhibited before him in the afternoon the poised [standing] racehorses.

31.author: Sarwar
31.text: When the noble galloping horses were displayed to him one evening,

31.author: Shakir
31.text: When there were brought to him in the evening (horses) still when standing, swift when running--

31.author: Wahiduddin Khan
31.text: When well-bred horses, which were fleet of foot were paraded before him near the close of day,

31.author: Yusuf Ali
31.text: Behold, there were brought before him, at eventide coursers of the highest breeding, and swift of foot;

31.author: Transliteration
31.text: Ith AAurida AAalayhi bialAAashiyyi alssafinatu aljiyadu

32.author: Ahmed Ali
32.text: He said: "The love of horses is worthy of desire to me for the remembrance of my Lord;" and when they were out of sight, (he said):

32.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
32.text: Therefore Sulaiman said, “I cherish the love of these horses*, out of remembrance of my Lord”; he then ordered them to be raced until they vanished in a curtain out of sight. (To be used in holy war.)

32.author: Arberry
32.text: he said, 'Lo, I have loved the love of good things better than the remembrance of my Lord, until the sun was bidden behind the veil.

32.author: Daryabadi
32.text: He said: verily I have loved the love of earthly good above the remembrance of my Lord until the sun hath disappeared behind the veil.

32.author: Hilali & Khan
32.text: And he said: "Alas! I did love the good (these horses) instead of remembering my Lord (in my 'Asr prayer)" till the time was over, and (the sun) had hidden in the veil (of night).

32.author: Itani
32.text: He said, “I have preferred the love of niceties to the remembrance of my Lord—until it disappeared behind the veil.

32.author: Maududi
32.text: he said: “Lo! I have come to love this wealth on account of the remembrance of my Lord.” And when the horses disappeared,

32.author: Mubarakpuri
32.text: He said: "I did love the good instead of remembering my Lord," till the time was over, and (the sun) had hidden in the veil (of night).

32.author: Pickthall
32.text: And he said: Lo! I have preferred the good things (of the world) to the remembrance of my Lord; till they were taken out of sight behind the curtain.

32.author: Qarai
32.text: he said, ‘Indeed I have preferred the love of [worldly] niceties to the remembrance of my Lord until [the sun] disappeared behind the [night’s] veil.’

32.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
32.text: he said: 'Indeed I have loved the love of good things better than the remembrance of my Lord until the sun has vanished behind a veil.

32.author: Saheeh International
32.text: And he said, "Indeed, I gave preference to the love of good [things] over the remembrance of my Lord until the sun disappeared into the curtain [of darkness]."

32.author: Sarwar
32.text: he said, "My love of horses for the cause of God has made me continue watching them until sunset, thus making me miss my prayer".

32.author: Shakir
32.text: Then he said: Surely I preferred the good things to the remembrance of my Lord-- until the sun set and time for Asr prayer was over, (he said):

32.author: Wahiduddin Khan
32.text: he said, "I have put the love of good things above the remembrance of my Lord," until [the sun] disappeared behind its veil and the horses disappeared from sight,

32.author: Yusuf Ali
32.text: And he said, "Truly do I love the love of good, with a view to the glory of my Lord,"- until (the sun) was hidden in the veil (of night):

32.author: Transliteration
32.text: Faqala innee ahbabtu hubba alkhayri AAan thikri rabbee hatta tawarat bialhijabi

33.author: Ahmed Ali
33.text: "Bring them back to me," and he began to rub and stroke their shanks and necks.

33.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
33.text: He then ordered, “Bring them back to me”; and he began caressing their shins and necks.

33.author: Arberry
33.text: Return them to me!' And he began to stroke their shanks and necks.

33.author: Daryabadi
33.text: Bring them back Unto me; and he set about slashing their legs and necks.

33.author: Hilali & Khan
33.text: Then he said "Bring them (horses) back to me." Then he began to pass his hand over their legs and their necks (till the end of the display).

33.author: Itani
33.text: Bring them back to me.” And he began caressing their legs and necks.

33.author: Maududi
33.text: (he ordered): “Bring these horses back to me,” and then he began to gently stroke their shanks and necks.

33.author: Mubarakpuri
33.text: Then he said: "Bring them back to me." Then he began to pass his hand over their legs and their necks.

33.author: Pickthall
33.text: (Then he said): Bring them back to me, and fell to slashing (with his sword their) legs and necks.

33.author: Qarai
33.text: ‘Bring it back for me!’ Then he [and others] began to wipe [their] legs and necks.

33.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
33.text: Bring them back to me' And he hacked their legs and necks (slaughtering them for Allah).

33.author: Saheeh International
33.text: [He said], "Return them to me," and set about striking [their] legs and necks.

33.author: Sarwar
33.text: He said, "Bring them back to me." Then he started to rub their legs and necks.

33.author: Shakir
33.text: Bring them back to me; so he began to slash (their) legs and necks.

33.author: Wahiduddin Khan
33.text: "Bring them back to me!" -- [he said] and began to stroke their legs and their necks.

33.author: Yusuf Ali
33.text: "Bring them back to me." then began he to pass his hand over (their) legs and their necks.

33.author: Transliteration
33.text: Ruddooha AAalayya fatafiqa mashan bialssooqi waalaAAnaqi

34.author: Ahmed Ali
34.text: We surely tried Solomon, and placed another body on his throne. So he turned to God

34.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
34.text: And We indeed tested Sulaiman, and placed a dead body on his throne – he therefore inclined towards His Lord.

34.author: Arberry
34.text: Certainly We tried Solomon, and We cast upon his throne a mere body; then he repented.

34.author: Daryabadi
34.text: And assuredly We tried Sulaiman, and set upon his throne a mere body. Thereafter he was penitent.

34.author: Hilali & Khan
34.text: And, indeed We did try Sulaiman (Solomon) and We placed on his throne Jasadan (a devil, so he lost his kingdom for a while) but he did return (to his throne and kingdom by the Grace of Allah and he did return) to Allah with obedience and in repentance.

34.author: Itani
34.text: We tested Solomon, and placed a body on his throne; then he repented.

34.author: Maududi
34.text: Surely We put Solomon to the test and cast upon his throne a mere body. Thereupon he penitently turned (to Us).

34.author: Mubarakpuri
34.text: And indeed, We tried Sulayman and We placed on his throne Jasad (a body), and he returned.

34.author: Pickthall
34.text: And verily We tried Solomon, and set upon his throne a (mere) body. Then did he repent.

34.author: Qarai
34.text: Certainly We tried Solomon, and cast a [lifeless] body on his throne. Thereupon he was penitent.

34.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
34.text: Indeed, We tried Solomon and placed a body (of a child) upon his throne, then he repented.

34.author: Saheeh International
34.text: And We certainly tried Solomon and placed on his throne a body; then he returned.

34.author: Sarwar
34.text: We tested Solomon by (causing death to his son) and leaving his body on Solomon's chair. Then he turned to Us in repentance,

34.author: Shakir
34.text: And certainly We tried Sulaiman, and We put on his throne a (mere) body, so he turned (to Allah).

34.author: Wahiduddin Khan
34.text: We tried Solomon by placing upon his throne a [lifeless] body; and thereupon he turned towards Us].

34.author: Yusuf Ali
34.text: And We did try Solomon: We placed on his throne a body (without life); but he did turn (to Us in true devotion):

34.author: Transliteration
34.text: Walaqad fatanna sulaymana waalqayna AAala kursiyyihi jasadan thumma anaba

35.author: Ahmed Ali
35.text: Saying: "O Lord, forgive me, and give me such a dominion as none will merit after me. You are the great bestower."

35.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
35.text: He said, “My Lord! Forgive me and bestow upon me a kingdom, which shall not befit anyone after me; indeed only You are the Great Bestower.”

35.author: Arberry
35.text: He said, 'My Lord, forgive me, and give me a kingdom such as may not befall anyone after me; surely Thou art the All-giver.'

35.author: Daryabadi
35.text: He said: my Lord! forgive me, and bestow on me a dominion which no one may obtain beside me: verily Thou! Thou art the Bestower.

35.author: Hilali & Khan
35.text: He said: "My Lord! Forgive me, and bestow upon me a kingdom such as shall not belong to any other after me: Verily, You are the Bestower."

35.author: Itani
35.text: He said, “My Lord, forgive me, and grant me a kingdom never to be attained by anyone after me. You are the Giver.”

35.author: Maududi
35.text: He said: “My Lord, forgive me and bestow upon me a kingdom such as none other after me will deserve. Surely You are the Bounteous Giver.”

35.author: Mubarakpuri
35.text: He said: "My Lord! Forgive me, and bestow upon me a kingdom such as shall not belong to any other after me. Verily, You are the Bestower."

35.author: Pickthall
35.text: He said: My Lord! Forgive me and bestow on me sovereignty such as shall not belong to any after me. Lo! Thou art the Bestower.

35.author: Qarai
35.text: He said, ‘My Lord! Forgive me, and grant me a kingdom that will not befit anyone except me. Indeed You are the All-munificent.’

35.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
35.text: He said: 'Forgive me my Lord, and give me a kingdom the like of which will not befall any after me, surely, You are the Giver'

35.author: Saheeh International
35.text: He said, "My Lord, forgive me and grant me a kingdom such as will not belong to anyone after me. Indeed, You are the Bestower."

35.author: Sarwar
35.text: saying, "Lord, forgive me and grant me a kingdom that no one after me can have the like. You are All-munificent".

35.author: Shakir
35.text: He said: My Lord! do Thou forgive me and grant me a kingdom which is not fit for (being inherited by) anyone after me;

35.author: Wahiduddin Khan
35.text: He prayed, "Lord forgive me! Grant me such power as no one after me will have -- You are the Most Generous Provider."

35.author: Yusuf Ali
35.text: He said, "O my Lord! Forgive me, and grant me a kingdom which, (it may be), suits not another after me: for Thou art the Grantor of Bounties (without measure).

35.author: Transliteration
35.text: Qala rabbi ighfir lee wahab lee mulkan la yanbaghee liahadin min baAAdee innaka anta alwahhabu

36.author: Ahmed Ali
36.text: So we subjugated the wind to his service which carried his merchandise wheresoever he wished;

36.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
36.text: We therefore gave the wind under his control, moving steadily by his command wherever he wished.

36.author: Arberry
36.text: So We subjected to him the wind, that ran at his commandment, softly, wherever he might light on,

36.author: Daryabadi
36.text: Then We subjected to him the wind: it ran gently by his command withersoever he directed.

36.author: Hilali & Khan
36.text: So, We subjected to him the wind, it blew gently to his order whithersoever he willed,

36.author: Itani
36.text: So We placed the wind at his service, blowing gently by his command, wherever he directed.

36.author: Maududi
36.text: We subjected the wind to him, so that it blew gently at his bidding, wherever he directed it,

36.author: Mubarakpuri
36.text: So, We subjected to him the wind; it blew gently by his order wherever he willed,

36.author: Pickthall
36.text: So We made the wind subservient unto him, setting fair by his command whithersoever he intended.

36.author: Qarai
36.text: So We disposed the wind for him, blowing softly wherever he intended by his command,

36.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
36.text: So We subjected the wind to him, so that it ran softly by his command wherever he wished;

36.author: Saheeh International
36.text: So We subjected to him the wind blowing by his command, gently, wherever he directed,

36.author: Sarwar
36.text: We made the wind subservient to him, to blow gently wherever he desired at his command

36.author: Shakir
36.text: Then We made the wind subservient to him; it made his command to run gently wherever he desired,

36.author: Wahiduddin Khan
36.text: Then We subjected the wind to his power, so that it blew gently, at his behest, wherever he willed,

36.author: Yusuf Ali
36.text: Then We subjected the wind to his power, to flow gently to his order, Whithersoever he willed,-

36.author: Transliteration
36.text: Fasakhkharna lahu alrreeha tajree biamrihi rukhaan haythu asaba

37.author: Ahmed Ali
37.text: And the devils -- the builders and divers of all kinds,

37.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
37.text: And made the demons subservient to him, all builders and divers.

37.author: Arberry
37.text: and the Satans, every builder and diver

37.author: Daryabadi
37.text: And We subjected to him the evil ones: every builder and diver.

37.author: Hilali & Khan
37.text: And also the Shayatin (devils) from the jinns (including) every kind of builder and diver,

37.author: Itani
37.text: And the demons—every builder and diver.

37.author: Maududi
37.text: and We also subjected the devils to him -- all kinds of builders and divers;

37.author: Mubarakpuri
37.text: And the Shayatin, from every kind of builder and diver,

37.author: Pickthall
37.text: And the unruly, every builder and diver (made We subservient),

37.author: Qarai
37.text: and every builder and diver from the demons,

37.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
37.text: and the satans, every builder and diver

37.author: Saheeh International
37.text: And [also] the devils [of jinn] - every builder and diver

37.author: Sarwar
37.text: and all the devils who built and dived for him.

37.author: Shakir
37.text: And the shaitans, every builder and diver,

37.author: Wahiduddin Khan
37.text: and also the jinn, every kind of builder and diver

37.author: Yusuf Ali
37.text: As also the evil ones, (including) every kind of builder and diver,-

38.author: Ahmed Ali
38.text: And many others bound in bond.

38.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
38.text: And other demons bound in chains.

38.author: Arberry
38.text: and others also, coupled in fetters:

38.author: Hilali & Khan
38.text: And also others bound in fetters.

38.author: Maududi
38.text: and others that were bound with chains.

38.author: Mubarakpuri
38.text: And also others bound in fetters.

38.author: Pickthall
38.text: And others linked together in chains,

38.author: Qarai
38.text: and others [too] bound together in chains.

38.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
38.text: and others joined together by (iron) fetters.

38.author: Saheeh International
38.text: And others bound together in shackles.

38.author: Sarwar
38.text: The rest of the devils were bound in chains.

38.author: Yusuf Ali
38.text: As also others bound together in fetters.

39.author: Ahmed Ali
39.text: "This is Our gift," (We said to him), "so bestow freely or withhold without reckoning."

39.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
39.text: “This is Our bestowal – you may therefore bestow favours or withhold them – you will not be questioned.”

39.author: Arberry
39.text: 'This is Our gift; bestow or withhold without reckoning.'

39.author: Daryabadi
39.text: This is Our gift, SO bestow thou or withhold, without rendering an account.

39.author: Hilali & Khan
39.text: [Saying of Allah to Sulaiman (Solomon)]: "This is Our gift, so spend you or withhold, no account will be asked."

39.author: Itani
39.text: “This is Our gift; so give generously, or withhold; without account.”

39.author: Maududi
39.text: “This is Our bestowal. So give or withhold as you wish without account.”

39.author: Mubarakpuri
39.text: [Allah said to Sulayman]: "This is Our gift, so spend you or withhold, no account will be asked of you."

39.author: Pickthall
39.text: (Saying): This is Our gift, so bestow thou, or withhold, without reckoning.

39.author: Qarai
39.text: ‘This is Our bounty: so withhold or bestow without any reckoning.’

39.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
39.text: 'This is Our gift, give or withhold without reckoning'

39.author: Saheeh International
39.text: [We said], "This is Our gift, so grant or withhold without account."

39.author: Sarwar
39.text: We told him, "This is Our gift to you so give them away free or keep them as you like".

39.author: Shakir
39.text: This is Our free gift, therefore give freely or withhold, without reckoning.

39.author: Wahiduddin Khan
39.text: We said: "This is Our gift, so give or withhold as you wish without reckoning."

39.author: Yusuf Ali
39.text: "Such are Our Bounties: whether thou bestow them (on others) or withhold them, no account will be asked."

39.author: Transliteration
39.text: Hatha AAataona faomnun aw amsik bighayri hisabin

40.author: Ahmed Ali
40.text: He has a high position with Us and an excellent abode.

40.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
40.text: And indeed for him in Our presence are, surely, proximity and an excellent abode.

40.author: Arberry
40.text: And he had a near place in Our presence and a fair resort.

40.author: Daryabadi
40.text: And verily for him is an approach with us, and a happy end.

40.author: Hilali & Khan
40.text: And verily, he enjoyed a near access to Us, and a good final return (Paradise).

40.author: Itani
40.text: For him is nearness to Us, and a beautiful resort.

40.author: Maududi
40.text: Indeed an exalted position of nearness awaits him and an excellent resort.

40.author: Mubarakpuri
40.text: And verily, for him is a near access to Us, and a good (final) return.

40.author: Pickthall
40.text: And lo! he hath favour with Us, and a happy journey's end.

40.author: Qarai
40.text: Indeed he has [a station of] nearness with Us and a good destination.

40.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
40.text: And he has a place near to Us, and a fine return.

40.author: Saheeh International
40.text: And indeed, for him is nearness to Us and a good place of return.

40.author: Sarwar
40.text: In Our eyes he certainly has a high position and the best place to return.

40.author: Shakir
40.text: And most surely he had a nearness to Us and an excellent resort.

40.author: Wahiduddin Khan
40.text: His reward will be nearness to Us, a good place to return to.

40.author: Yusuf Ali
40.text: And he enjoyed, indeed, a Near Approach to Us, and a beautiful Place of (Final) Return.

40.author: Transliteration
40.text: Wainna lahu AAindana lazulfa wahusna maabin

41.author: Ahmed Ali
41.text: Remember Our votary Job because he called to his Lord: "Satan has afflicted me with disease and distress."

41.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
41.text: And remember Our bondman Ayyub (Job); when he cried out* to his Lord, “The devil has struck me with hardship and pain.” (After seven years of patience.)

41.author: Arberry
41.text: Remember also Our servant Job; when he called to his Lord, 'Behold, Satan has visited me with weariness and chastisement.'

41.author: Daryabadi
41.text: And remember thou Our bondman Ayyub, what time he cried Unto his Lord: verily the Satan hath touched me with affliction and suffering.

41.author: Hilali & Khan
41.text: And remember Our slave Ayub (Job), when he invoked his Lord (saying): "Verily! Shaitan (Satan) has touched me with distress (by losing my health) and torment (by losing my wealth)!

41.author: Itani
41.text: And mention Our servant Job, when he called out to his Lord, “Satan has afflicted me with hardship and pain.”

41.author: Maududi
41.text: And remember Our servant Job: when he cried to his Lord: “Behold, Satan has afflicted me with much hardship and suffering.”

41.author: Mubarakpuri
41.text: And remember Our servant Ayyub, when he invoked his Lord (saying): "Verily, Shaytan has afflicted me with distress and torment!"

41.author: Pickthall
41.text: And make mention (O Muhammad) of Our bondman Job, when he cried unto his Lord (saying): Lo! the devil doth afflict me with distress and torment.

41.author: Qarai
41.text: And remember Our servant Job [in the Quran]. When he called out to his Lord, ‘The devil has visited on me hardship and torment,’

41.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
41.text: Also, remember Our worshiper Job. He called out to his Lord, (saying): 'satan has afflicted me with harm and pain'

41.author: Saheeh International
41.text: And remember Our servant Job, when he called to his Lord, "Indeed, Satan has touched me with hardship and torment."

41.author: Sarwar
41.text: (Muhammad), recall Our servant Job. When he prayed to his Lord saying, "Satan has afflicted me with hardship and torment,"

41.author: Shakir
41.text: And remember Our servant Ayyub, when he called upon his Lord: The Shaitan has afflicted me with toil and torment.

41.author: Wahiduddin Khan
41.text: Bring to mind Our servant Job who cried to his Lord, "Satan has afflicted me with distress and suffering."

41.author: Yusuf Ali
41.text: Commemorate Our Servant Job. Behold he cried to his Lord: "The Evil One has afflicted me with distress and suffering!"

41.author: Transliteration
41.text: Waothkur AAabdana ayyooba ith nada rabbahu annee massaniya alshshaytanu binusbin waAAathabin

42.author: Ahmed Ali
42.text: "Go swiftly to the spring," (We said). "This cold water is for bathing and for drinking."

42.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
42.text: We said to him, “Strike the earth with your foot; this cool spring is for bathing and drinking.” (A spring of gushed forth when he struck the earth – this was a miracle.)

42.author: Arberry
42.text: 'Stamp thy foot! This is a laving-place cool, and a drink.'

42.author: Daryabadi
42.text: Stamp the ground with thy foot. yonder is water to wash in, cool, and water to drink.

42.author: Hilali & Khan
42.text: (Allah said to him): "Strike the ground with your foot: This is a spring of water to wash in, cool and a (refreshing) drink."

42.author: Itani
42.text: “Stamp with your foot—here is cool water to wash with, and to drink.”

42.author: Maududi
42.text: (We commanded him): “Stamp your foot on earth, and here is cool water to wash with and to drink.”

42.author: Mubarakpuri
42.text: (Allah said to him): "Strike the ground with your foot. This is (a spring of) water to wash in, cool and a drink."

42.author: Pickthall
42.text: (And it was said unto him): Strike the ground with thy foot. This (spring) is a cool bath and a refreshing drink.

42.author: Qarai
42.text: [We told him:] ‘Stamp your foot on the ground; this [ensuing spring] will be a cooling bath and drink.’

42.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
42.text: (We said): 'Stamp your foot on the ground, here is cool water with which to wash and a drink'

42.author: Saheeh International
42.text: [So he was told], "Strike [the ground] with your foot; this is a [spring for] a cool bath and drink."

42.author: Sarwar
42.text: (We answered his prayer, healed his sickness, and told him), "Run on your feet. This is cool water (for you) to wash and drink".

42.author: Shakir
42.text: Urge with your foot; here is a cool washing-place and a drink.

42.author: Wahiduddin Khan
42.text: We said, "Stamp your foot! Here is cool water for you to wash in and drink,"

42.author: Yusuf Ali
42.text: (The command was given:) "Strike with thy foot: here is (water) wherein to wash, cool and refreshing, and (water) to drink."

42.author: Transliteration
42.text: Orkud birijlika hatha mughtasalun baridun washarabun

43.author: Ahmed Ali
43.text: We restored his family to him with others similar to them, as a blessing from Us and a reminder for men of wisdom. --

43.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
43.text: And We bestowed his household to him and one more similar to it – as a mercy from Us, and as a remembrance for the people of intellect.

43.author: Arberry
43.text: And We gave to him his family, and the like of them with them, as a mercy from us, and a reminder unto men possessed of minds;

43.author: Daryabadi
43.text: And We bestowed on him his household and along with them the like thereof, out of mercy from us, and a remembrance Unto men of understanding.

43.author: Hilali & Khan
43.text: And We gave him (back) his family, and along with them the like thereof, as a Mercy from Us, and a Reminder for those who understand.

43.author: Itani
43.text: And We restored his family for him, and their like with them; as a mercy from Us, and a lesson for those who possess insight.

43.author: Maududi
43.text: And We granted to him his family and also the like of them, as a mercy from Us, and as a reminder to people of understanding,

43.author: Mubarakpuri
43.text: And We gave him (back) his family, and along with them the like thereof, as a mercy from Us, and a reminder for those who understand.

43.author: Pickthall
43.text: And We bestowed on him (again) his household and therewith the like thereof, a mercy from Us, and a memorial for men of understanding.

43.author: Qarai
43.text: We gave [back] his family to him along with others like them, as a mercy from Us and an admonition for those who possess intellect.

43.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
43.text: We restored to him to his family and like those with them, a mercy from Us and a reminder to a nation that understand.

43.author: Saheeh International
43.text: And We granted him his family and a like [number] with them as mercy from Us and a reminder for those of understanding.

43.author: Sarwar
43.text: We gave him back his family and doubled their number as a blessing from Us and as a reminder to the people of understanding.

43.author: Shakir
43.text: And We gave him his family and the like of them with them, as a mercy from Us, and as a reminder to those possessed of understanding.

43.author: Wahiduddin Khan
43.text: We restored his family to him, doubling their number as an act of grace from Us, and as a reminder to all who are endowed with insight.

43.author: Yusuf Ali
43.text: And We gave him (back) his people, and doubled their number,- as a Grace from Ourselves, and a thing for commemoration, for all who have Understanding.

43.author: Transliteration
43.text: Wawahabna lahu ahlahu wamithlahum maAAahum rahmatan minna wathikra liolee alalbabi

44.author: Ahmed Ali
44.text: "Take a handful of herbs," (We said to him), and apply and rub them, and do not make a mistake." We found him patient in adversity, an excellent devotee, always turning in repentance.

44.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
44.text: And We said, “Take a broom in your hand and strike her with it, and do not break your vow”; We indeed found him patiently enduring; what an excellent bondman! He is indeed most inclined.

44.author: Arberry
44.text: and, 'Take in thy hand a bundle of rushes, and strike therewith, and do not fail in thy oath.' Surely We found him a steadfast man. How excellent a servant he was! He was a penitent.

44.author: Daryabadi
44.text: And take in thine hand a handful of twigs, and strike therewith, and break not thine oath. Verily We! We found him patient. How excellent a bondman! Verily he was oft-returning.

44.author: Hilali & Khan
44.text: "And take in your hand a bundle of thin grass and strike therewith (your wife), and break not your oath. Truly! We found him patient. How excellent (a) slave! Verily, he was ever oft-returning in repentance (to Us)!

44.author: Itani
44.text: “Take with your hand a bundle, and strike with it, and do not break your oath.” We found him patient. What an excellent servant! He was obedient.

44.author: Maududi
44.text: (and We said to him): “Take in your hand a bundle of rushes and strike with it, and do not break your oath.” Indeed We found him steadfast. How excellent a servant (of Ours) he was. Indeed he constantly turned (to his Lord).

44.author: Mubarakpuri
44.text: "And take in your hand a bundle of thin grass and strike therewith (your wife), and break not your oath." Truly, We found him patient. How excellent a servant! Verily, he was ever oft-returning in repentance (to Us)!

44.author: Pickthall
44.text: And (it was said unto him): Take in thine hand a branch and smite therewith, and break not thine oath. Lo! We found him steadfast, how excellent a slave! Lo! he was ever turning in repentance (to his Lord).

44.author: Qarai
44.text: [We told him:] ‘Take a faggot in your hand and then strike [your wife] with it, but do not break [your] oath.’ Indeed, We found him to be patient. What an excellent servant! Indeed he was a penitent [soul].

44.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
44.text: (We said to him): 'Take a bundle of rushes and strike with it; and do not break your oath' We found him to be patient, a good worshiper and he was penitent.

44.author: Saheeh International
44.text: [We said], "And take in your hand a bunch [of grass] and strike with it and do not break your oath." Indeed, We found him patient, an excellent servant. Indeed, he was one repeatedly turning back [to Allah].

44.author: Sarwar
44.text: We told him, "Take a handful of straw. Strike your wife with it to fulfill your oath." We found him to be patient. What an excellent servant he was. He was certainly most repenting.

44.author: Shakir
44.text: And take in your hand a green branch and beat her with It and do not break your oath; surely We found him patient; most excellent the servant! Surely he was frequent in returning (to Allah).

44.author: Wahiduddin Khan
44.text: We said to Him, "Take a handful of twigs in your hand and strike with that but do not break your oath." We found him steadfast. What an excellent servant! He turned constantly to his Lord.

44.author: Yusuf Ali
44.text: "And take in thy hand a little grass, and strike therewith: and break not (thy oath)." Truly We found him full of patience and constancy. How excellent in Our service! ever did he turn (to Us)!

44.author: Transliteration
44.text: Wakhuth biyadika dighthan faidrib bihi wala tahnath inna wajadnahu sabiran niAAma alAAabdu innahu awwabun

45.author: Ahmed Ali
45.text: Remember Our votaries Abraham, Isaac and Jacob, men of power and insight.

45.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
45.text: And remember Our bondmen Ibrahim, and Ishaq, and Yaqub – the men of power and knowledge.

45.author: Arberry
45.text: Remember also Our servants Abraham, Isaac and Jacob -- men of might they and of vision.

45.author: Daryabadi
45.text: And remember thou Our bond men, Ibrahim and Is-haq and Ya'qub. owners of might ana insight.

45.author: Hilali & Khan
45.text: And remember Our slaves, Ibrahim (Abraham), Ishaque (Isaac), and Ya'qub (Jacob), (all) owners of strength (in worshipping Us) and (also) of religious understanding.

45.author: Itani
45.text: And mention Our servants Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob—endowed with ability and vision.

45.author: Maududi
45.text: And remember Our servants -- Abraham, Isaac and Jacob -- they were endowed with great strength and vision.

45.author: Mubarakpuri
45.text: And remember Our servants, Ibrahim, Ishaq, and Ya`qub, Ulil-Aydi wal-Absar.

45.author: Pickthall
45.text: And make mention of Our bondmen, Abraham, Isaac and Jacob, men of parts and vision.

45.author: Qarai
45.text: And remember Our servants, Abraham, Isaac and Jacob, men of strength and insight.

45.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
45.text: Also, remember Our worshipers Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, those of might and vision.

45.author: Saheeh International
45.text: And remember Our servants, Abraham, Isaac and Jacob - those of strength and [religious] vision.

45.author: Sarwar
45.text: (Muhammad), recall Our servants Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, all of whom possessed virtuous hands and clear visions.

45.author: Shakir
45.text: And remember Our servants Ibrahim and Ishaq and Yaqoub, men of power and insight.

45.author: Wahiduddin Khan
45.text: Remember Our servants Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob -- possessors of strength and vision.

45.author: Yusuf Ali
45.text: And commemorate Our Servants Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, possessors of Power and Vision.

45.author: Transliteration
45.text: Waothkur AAibadana ibraheema waishaqa wayaAAqooba olee alaydee waalabsari

46.author: Ahmed Ali
46.text: We distinguished them for the distinct remembrance of the abode (of the Hereafter).

46.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
46.text: We indeed gave them distinction with a genuine affair – the remembrance of the (everlasting) abode.

46.author: Arberry
46.text: Assuredly We purified them with a quality most pure, the remembrance of the Abode,

46.author: Daryabadi
46.text: Verily We! We distinguished them with a distinct quality: the remembrance of the Abode.

46.author: Hilali & Khan
46.text: Verily, We did choose them by granting them (a good thing, i.e.) the remembrance of the home [in the Hereafter and they used to make the people remember it, and also they used to invite the people to obey Allah and to do good deeds for the Hereafter].

46.author: Itani
46.text: We distinguished them with a distinct quality: the remembrance of the Home.

46.author: Maududi
46.text: Verily We exalted them in consideration of a sterling quality: their remembrance of the Abode of the Hereafter.

46.author: Mubarakpuri
46.text: Verily, We did choose them by granting them the remembrance of the Abode.

46.author: Pickthall
46.text: Lo! We purified them with a pure thought, remembrance of the Home (of the Hereafter).

46.author: Qarai
46.text: Indeed We purified them with exclusive remembrance of the abode [of the Hereafter].

46.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
46.text: Indeed, We purified them with a most pure quality, the remembrance of the Everlasting Life.

46.author: Saheeh International
46.text: Indeed, We chose them for an exclusive quality: remembrance of the home [of the Hereafter].

46.author: Sarwar
46.text: We gave them this pure distinction because of their continual remembrance of the Day of Judgment.

46.author: Shakir
46.text: Surely We purified them by a pure quality, the keeping in mind of the (final) abode.

46.author: Wahiduddin Khan
46.text: We chose them for a special [purpose] -- proclaiming the message of the Hereafter:

46.author: Yusuf Ali
46.text: Verily We did choose them for a special (purpose)- proclaiming the Message of the Hereafter.

47.author: Ahmed Ali
47.text: They are the chosen ones, the excellent in Our sight.

47.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
47.text: And in Our sight, they are indeed the chosen ones, the beloved.

47.author: Arberry
47.text: and in Our sight they are of the chosen, the excellent.

47.author: Daryabadi
47.text: And verily they are with us of the elect of the excellent ones

47.author: Hilali & Khan
47.text: And they are with Us, verily, of the chosen and the best!

47.author: Itani
47.text: To Us they are among the chosen, the outstanding.

47.author: Maududi
47.text: In Our sight they are among the chosen and excellent ones.

47.author: Mubarakpuri
47.text: And they are with Us, verily, of the chosen and the best!

47.author: Pickthall
47.text: Lo! in Our sight they are verily of the elect, the excellent.

47.author: Qarai
47.text: Indeed they are surely with Us among the elect of the best.

47.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
47.text: Indeed with Us they are among the chosen, the excellent.

47.author: Saheeh International
47.text: And indeed they are, to Us, among the chosen and outstanding.

47.author: Sarwar
47.text: In Our eyes they were of the chosen, virtuous people.

47.author: Shakir
47.text: And most surely they were with Us, of the elect, the best.

47.author: Wahiduddin Khan
47.text: and, in Our sight they were indeed among the select, the truly good!

47.author: Yusuf Ali
47.text: They were, in Our sight, truly, of the company of the Elect and the Good.

48.author: Ahmed Ali
48.text: And remember Ishmael, Elisha and Dhu'l-Kifl. Every one of them is among the best.

48.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
48.text: And remember Ismail and Yasa’a (Elisha) and Zul-Kifl; and they are all excellent.

48.author: Arberry
48.text: Remember also Our servants Ishmael, Elisha, and Dhul Kifl; each is among the excellent.

48.author: Daryabadi
48.text: And remember Isma'il and Al-Yas'a and Zul-kifl: all of the excellent ones.

48.author: Hilali & Khan
48.text: And remember Isma'il (Ishmael), Al-Yasa'a (Elisha), and Dhul-Kifl (Isaiah), all are among the best.

48.author: Itani
48.text: And mention Ishmael, Elisha, and Ezekiel; all are among the outstanding.

48.author: Maududi
48.text: And remember Ishmael, Elisha, and Dhu al-Kifl. All were of the best.

48.author: Mubarakpuri
48.text: And remember Isma`il, Al-Yasa`, and Dhul-Kifl, all are among the best.

48.author: Pickthall
48.text: And make mention of Ishmael and Elisha and Dhu'l-Kifl. All are of the chosen.

48.author: Qarai
48.text: And remember Ishmael, Elisha and Dhu’l-Kifl—each [of whom was] among the elect.

48.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
48.text: Also, remember our worshipers Ishmael, Elisha, and ThulKifl, they are among the good.

48.author: Saheeh International
48.text: And remember Ishmael, Elisha and Dhul-Kifl, and all are among the outstanding.

48.author: Sarwar
48.text: Recall Ismael, Elisha, and Dhulkifl (Ezekiel) who were all virtuous people.

48.author: Shakir
48.text: And remember Ismail and Al-Yasha and Zulkifl; and they were all of the best.

48.author: Wahiduddin Khan
48.text: Remember [Our servants] Ishmael, Elisha, and Dhu'l-Kifl. Each of them was among the just.

48.author: Yusuf Ali
48.text: And commemorate Isma'il, Elisha, and Zul-Kifl: Each of them was of the Company of the Good.

48.author: Transliteration
48.text: Waothkur ismaAAeela wailyasaAAa watha alkifli wakullun mina alakhyari

49.author: Ahmed Ali
49.text: This is a commemoration. Surely for those who take heed for themselves is an excellent place of return --

49.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
49.text: This is an advice; and indeed for the pious is an excellent abode.

49.author: Arberry
49.text: This is a Remembrance; and for the godfearing is a fair resort,

49.author: Daryabadi
49.text: This is an admonition, and verily for the God-feoring is a happy retreat:

49.author: Hilali & Khan
49.text: This is a Reminder, and verily, for the Muttaqun (pious and righteous persons - see V. 2:2) is a good final return (Paradise),

49.author: Itani
49.text: This is a reminder. The devout will have a good place of return.

49.author: Maududi
49.text: This was a remembrance. An excellent retreat awaits the God-fearing

49.author: Mubarakpuri
49.text: This is a Reminder. And verily, for those who have Taqwa is a good final return.

49.author: Pickthall
49.text: This is a reminder. And lo! for those who ward off (evil) is a happy journey's end,

49.author: Qarai
49.text: This is a Reminder, and indeed the Godwary have a good destination:

49.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
49.text: This is a Reminder, and indeed for the cautious is a fine return,

49.author: Saheeh International
49.text: This is a reminder. And indeed, for the righteous is a good place of return

49.author: Sarwar
49.text: Such is their noble story. The pious ones will certainly have the best place to return.

49.author: Shakir
49.text: This is a reminder; and most surely there is an excellent resort for those who guard (against evil),

49.author: Wahiduddin Khan
49.text: This is a Reminder. The righteous shall have a good place to return to:

49.author: Yusuf Ali
49.text: This is a Message (of admonition): and verily, for the righteous, is a beautiful Place of (Final) Return,-

49.author: Transliteration
49.text: Hatha thikrun wainna lilmuttaqeena lahusna maabin

50.author: Ahmed Ali
50.text: Gardens of Eden with gates open wide to them,

50.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
50.text: Everlasting Gardens – all its gates are open for them.

50.author: Arberry
50.text: Gardens of Eden, whereof the gates are open to them,

50.author: Daryabadi
50.text: Gardens Everlasting, whereof the portals remain opened for them.

50.author: Hilali & Khan
50.text: 'Adn (Edn) Paradise (everlasting Gardens), whose doors will be open for them, [It is said (in Tafsir At-Tabari, Part 23, Page 174) that one can speak to the doors, just one tells it to open and close, and it will open or close as it is ordered].

50.author: Itani
50.text: The Gardens of Eden, with their doors wide-open for them.

50.author: Maududi
50.text: -- everlasting Gardens with gates wide open for them

50.author: Mubarakpuri
50.text: `Adn Paradise, whose doors will be opened for them.

50.author: Pickthall
50.text: Gardens of Eden, whereof the gates are opened for them,

50.author: Qarai
50.text: the Gardens of Eden, whose gates will be flung open for them.

50.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
50.text: the Gardens of Eden whose gates shall be open to them,

50.author: Saheeh International
50.text: Gardens of perpetual residence, whose doors will be opened to them.

50.author: Sarwar
50.text: They will enter gardens of Eden with their gates open for them.

50.author: Shakir
50.text: The gardens of perpetuity, the doors are opened for them.

50.author: Wahiduddin Khan
50.text: the Gardens of eternity with gates thrown wide open to them.

50.author: Yusuf Ali
50.text: Gardens of Eternity, whose doors will (ever) be open to them;

51.author: Ahmed Ali
51.text: Where they will take their ease, calling for fruits in plenty, and for wine,

51.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
51.text: Reclining on pillows, in it they ask for fruits and drinks in plenty.

51.author: Arberry
51.text: wherein they recline, and wherein they call for fruits abundant, and sweet potions,

51.author: Daryabadi
51.text: Therein they will recline; therein they will call for plenteous fruit and drink.

51.author: Hilali & Khan
51.text: Therein they will recline; therein they will call for fruits in abundance and drinks;

51.author: Itani
51.text: Relaxing therein, and calling for abundant fruit and beverage.

51.author: Maududi
51.text: wherein they shall recline, wherein they shall ask for abundant fruit and drinks,

51.author: Mubarakpuri
51.text: Therein they will recline; therein they will call for fruits in abundance and drinks;

51.author: Pickthall
51.text: Wherein, reclining, they call for plenteous fruit and cool drink (that is) therein.

51.author: Qarai
51.text: Reclining therein [on couches], they will call for abundant fruits and drinks,

51.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
51.text: in which they will recline, and call for abundant fruit and drink therein.

51.author: Saheeh International
51.text: Reclining within them, they will call therein for abundant fruit and drink.

51.author: Sarwar
51.text: They will be resting therein and will be able to ask for many kinds of fruit and drink.

51.author: Shakir
51.text: Reclining therein, calling therein for many fruits and drink.

51.author: Wahiduddin Khan
51.text: They will be comfortably seated; reclining, they will call for abundant fruit and drink;

51.author: Yusuf Ali
51.text: Therein will they recline (at ease): Therein can they call (at pleasure) for fruit in abundance, and (delicious) drink;

51.author: Transliteration
51.text: Muttakieena feeha yadAAoona feeha bifakihatin katheeratin washarabin

52.author: Ahmed Ali
52.text: With companions of modest look, the same in age, by their side.

52.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
52.text: And with them are the pure spouses, who do not set gaze upon men except their husbands, of single age.

52.author: Arberry
52.text: and with them maidens restraining their glances of equal age.

52.author: Daryabadi
52.text: And with them will be virgins of refraining looks and of equal age.

52.author: Hilali & Khan
52.text: And beside them will be chaste females (virgins) restraining their glances only for their husbands, (and) of equal ages.

52.author: Itani
52.text: With them will be attendants with modest gaze, of same age.

52.author: Maududi
52.text: and wherein there shall be with them well-matched, bashful mates.

52.author: Mubarakpuri
52.text: And beside them will be Qasirat-at-Tarf, (and) of equal ages.

52.author: Pickthall
52.text: And with them are those of modest gaze, companions.

52.author: Qarai
52.text: and there will be with them maidens of restrained glances, of a like age.

52.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
52.text: And with them will be maidens of equal age with modest gaze.

52.author: Saheeh International
52.text: And with them will be women limiting [their] glances and of equal age.

52.author: Sarwar
52.text: They will have bashful wives of equal age with them.

52.author: Shakir
52.text: And with them shall be those restraining their eyes, equals in age.

52.author: Wahiduddin Khan
52.text: with them, they will have pure, modest women of an equal age.

52.author: Yusuf Ali
52.text: And beside them will be chaste women restraining their glances, (companions) of equal age.

53.author: Ahmed Ali
53.text: This is what is promised you for the Day of Reckoning.

53.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
53.text: This is the promise being given to you, for the Day of Reckoning.

53.author: Arberry
53.text: 'This is what you were promised for the Day of Reckoning;

53.author: Daryabadi
53.text: This it is that ye are promised for the Day of Reckoning.

53.author: Hilali & Khan
53.text: This it is what you (Al-Muttaqun - the pious) are promised for the Day of Reckoning!

53.author: Itani
53.text: This is what you are promised for the Day of Account.

53.author: Maududi
53.text: All this is what you are promised for the Day of Judgement.

53.author: Mubarakpuri
53.text: This it is what you are promised for the Day of Reckoning!

53.author: Pickthall
53.text: This it is that ye are promised for the Day of Reckoning.

53.author: Qarai
53.text: This is what you are promised on the Day of Reckoning.

53.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
53.text: 'This is what you are promised on the Day of Recompense,

53.author: Saheeh International
53.text: This is what you, [the righteous], are promised for the Day of Account.

53.author: Sarwar
53.text: This is what they had been promised for the Day of Judgment.

53.author: Shakir
53.text: This is what you are promised for the day of reckoning.

53.author: Wahiduddin Khan
53.text: This is what you were promised on the Day of Reckoning:

53.author: Yusuf Ali
53.text: Such is the Promise made, to you for the Day of Account!

54.author: Ahmed Ali
54.text: This is surely Our provision never-ending.

54.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
54.text: Indeed this is Our sustenance, which will never end.

54.author: Arberry
54.text: this is Our provision, unto which there is no end.'

54.author: Daryabadi
54.text: Verily this is Our provision: there will be no ceasing thereof.

54.author: Hilali & Khan
54.text: (It will be said to them)! Verily, this is Our Provision which will never finish;

54.author: Maududi
54.text: This is Our provision for you, never to end.

54.author: Mubarakpuri
54.text: (It will be said to them)! Verily, this is Our provision which will never finish.

54.author: Pickthall
54.text: Lo! this in truth is Our provision, which will never waste away.

54.author: Qarai
54.text: This is Our provision, which will never be exhausted.

54.author: Saheeh International
54.text: Indeed, this is Our provision; for it there is no depletion.

54.author: Sarwar
54.text: Our provision (for you) will never be exhausted.

54.author: Shakir
54.text: Most surely this is Our sustenance; it shall never come to an end;

54.author: Wahiduddin Khan
54.text: Our provision for you will never be exhausted.

54.author: Yusuf Ali
54.text: Truly such will be Our Bounty (to you); it will never fail;-

54.author: Transliteration
54.text: Inna hatha larizquna ma lahu min nafadin

55.author: Ahmed Ali
55.text: This (for the virtuous); but for the transgressors the evil destination,

55.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
55.text: This is for the virtuous; and indeed for the rebellious is a wretched destination.

55.author: Arberry
55.text: All this; but for the insolent awaits an ill resort,

55.author: Daryabadi
55.text: This: and verily for the exorbitant there shall be an evil retreat:

55.author: Hilali & Khan
55.text: This is so! And for the Taghun (transgressors, disobedient to Allah and His Messenger - disbelievers in the Oneness of Allah, criminals, etc.), will be an evil final return (Fire),

55.author: Itani
55.text: All This. But the transgressors will have a miserable return.

55.author: Maududi
55.text: All this (is for the God-fearing). But for the transgressors, an evil resort awaits them --

55.author: Mubarakpuri
55.text: This is so! And for the Taghin will be an evil final return.

55.author: Pickthall
55.text: This (is for the righteous). And lo! for the transgressors there with be an evil journey's end,

55.author: Qarai
55.text: This [will be for the righteous], and as for the rebellious there will surely be a bad destination:

55.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
55.text: All of this; but, for the proud there is an ill return.

55.author: Saheeh International
55.text: This [is so]. But indeed, for the transgressors is an evil place of return -

55.author: Sarwar
55.text: However, the rebellious ones will have the worst place to return.

55.author: Shakir
55.text: This (shall be so); and most surely there is an evil resort for the inordinate ones;

55.author: Wahiduddin Khan
55.text: But the arrogant will have the worst return:

55.author: Yusuf Ali
55.text: Yea, such! but - for the wrong-doers will be an evil place of (Final) Return!-

56.author: Ahmed Ali
56.text: Hell, in which they will burn. How vile a resting place!

56.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
56.text: Hell; which they shall enter; what an evil resting-place!

56.author: Arberry
56.text: Gehenna, wherein they are roasted - an evil cradling!

56.author: Daryabadi
56.text: Hell, wherein they roast: a wretched couch.

56.author: Hilali & Khan
56.text: Hell! Where they will burn, and worst (indeed) is that place to rest!

56.author: Itani
56.text: Hell; in which they will roast; what a miserable abode!

56.author: Maududi
56.text: Hell, where they will be roasted. An evil place to dwell!

56.author: Mubarakpuri
56.text: Hell! Where they will enter it, and worst (indeed) is that place to rest!

56.author: Pickthall
56.text: Hell, where they will burn, an evil resting-place.

56.author: Qarai
56.text: hell, which they shall enter, an evil resting place.

56.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
56.text: They shall roast in (the Fire) of Gehenna, an evil cradling.

56.author: Saheeh International
56.text: Hell, which they will [enter to] burn, and wretched is the resting place.

56.author: Sarwar
56.text: They will suffer in hell. What a terrible dwelling!

56.author: Shakir
56.text: Hell; they shall enter it, so evil is the resting-place.

56.author: Wahiduddin Khan
56.text: they will burn, in Hell, an evil resting place --

56.author: Yusuf Ali
56.text: Hell!- they will burn therein, - an evil bed (indeed, to lie on)!-

57.author: Ahmed Ali
57.text: There will be boiling water for them and cold, clammy, fetid drink to taste,

57.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
57.text: This is for the criminals – so that they may taste it – boiling hot water and pus.

57.author: Arberry
57.text: All this; so let them taste it - boiling water and pus,

57.author: Daryabadi
57.text: This - let them taste it: scalding water and corruption.

57.author: Hilali & Khan
57.text: This is so! Then let them taste it, a boiling fluid and dirty wound discharges.

57.author: Itani
57.text: All this. Let them taste it—boiling and bitter cold.

57.author: Maududi
57.text: All this (is for them); so let them taste boiling water and pus,

57.author: Mubarakpuri
57.text: This is so! Then let them taste it - Hamim and Ghassaq.

57.author: Pickthall
57.text: Here is a boiling and an ice-cold draught, so let them taste it,

57.author: Qarai
57.text: [They will be told, ‘This is scalding water and pus; let them taste it,

57.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
57.text: All of this; so let them taste it, boiling water and pus,

57.author: Saheeh International
57.text: This - so let them taste it - is scalding water and [foul] purulence.

57.author: Sarwar
57.text: (They will be told), "This is your recompense.

57.author: Shakir
57.text: This (shall be so); so let them taste it, boiling and intensely cold (drink).

57.author: Wahiduddin Khan
57.text: all this will be theirs; let them taste it -- a scalding, dark, foul fluid,

57.author: Yusuf Ali
57.text: Yea, such! - then shall they taste it,- a boiling fluid, and a fluid dark, murky, intensely cold!-

58.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
58.text: And similar other punishments in pairs.

58.author: Arberry
58.text: and other torments of the like kind coupled together.

58.author: Daryabadi
58.text: And other torments, like thereof, conjoined.

58.author: Hilali & Khan
58.text: And other torments of similar kind, all together!

58.author: Itani
58.text: And similar torments of diverse kinds.

58.author: Maududi
58.text: and other sufferings of the kind.

58.author: Mubarakpuri
58.text: And other of similar kind - all together!

58.author: Pickthall
58.text: And other (torment) of the kind in pairs (the two extremes)!

58.author: Qarai
58.text: and other kinds [of torments] resembling it.’

58.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
58.text: and other similar to it, joined together.

58.author: Saheeh International
58.text: And other [punishments] of its type [in various] kinds.

58.author: Sarwar
58.text: Taste the scalding water, pus, and other putrid things".

58.author: Shakir
58.text: And other (punishment) of the same kind-- of various sorts.

58.author: Yusuf Ali
58.text: And other Penalties of a similar kind, to match them!

59.author: Ahmed Ali
59.text: Here is a multitude rushing headlong with you. There is no welcome for them. They will roast in the fire.

59.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
59.text: “Here is another group that was with you, falling along with you”; they will answer, “Do not give them plenty of open space; they surely have to enter the fire – let them also be confined!”

59.author: Arberry
59.text: 'This is a troop rushing in with you; there is no Welcome for them; they shall roast in the Fire.'

59.author: Daryabadi
59.text: This is a crowd rushing in along with you; no welcome for them; verily they are to roast in the Fire.

59.author: Hilali & Khan
59.text: This is a troop entering with you (in Hell), no welcome for them! Verily, they shall burn in the Fire!

59.author: Itani
59.text: “This is a crowd rushing headlong with you.” There is no welcome for them. They will be scorched by the Fire.

59.author: Maududi
59.text: (Observing their followers advancing to Hell they will say, among themselves: “This is a troop rushing in to you. There is no welcome for them. They are destined to roast in the Fire.”

59.author: Mubarakpuri
59.text: This is a troop entering with you (in Hell), no welcome for them! Verily, they shall enter in the Fire!

59.author: Pickthall
59.text: Here is an army rushing blindly with you. (Those who are already in the Fire say): No word of welcome for them. Lo! they will roast at the Fire.

59.author: Qarai
59.text: [The leaders of the faithless will be told,] ‘This is a group [of your followers] plunging [into hell] along with you.’ [They will repond,] ‘May wretchedness be their lot! For they will enter the Fire.’

59.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
59.text: (We shall say to their leaders): 'This is a troop rushing in with you, there is no welcome for them, they shall roast in the Fire'

59.author: Saheeh International
59.text: [Its inhabitants will say], "This is a company bursting in with you. No welcome for them. Indeed, they will burn in the Fire."

59.author: Sarwar
59.text: Their leaders will be told, "This band will also be thrown headlong with you into hell." Their leaders will exclaim, "May condemnation fall upon them! Let them suffer the torment of fire".

59.author: Shakir
59.text: This is an army plunging in without consideration along with you; no welcome for them, surely they shall enter fire.

59.author: Wahiduddin Khan
59.text: [And they will say to one another: "Do you see] this crowd of people rushing headlong to join you?" "No welcome to them! Indeed, they are headed for the fire!"

59.author: Yusuf Ali
59.text: Here is a troop rushing headlong with you! No welcome for them! truly, they shall burn in the Fire!

59.author: Transliteration
59.text: Hatha fawjun muqtahimun maAAakum la marhaban bihim innahum saloo alnnari

60.author: Ahmed Ali
60.text: They will say: "In fact, it is you who will have no welcome. It is you who brought this upon us. What an evil place of rest!"

60.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
60.text: The followers will say, “In fact, for you! May you not get open space! It is you who brought this calamity upon us!” So what a wretched destination.

60.author: Arberry
60.text: They say, 'No, it is you have no Welcome; you forwarded it for us; how evil a stablishment!'

60.author: Daryabadi
60.text: They will say nay! it is ye, for whom there is no welcome: it is ye who have brought it upon us. Evil shall be the resting-place.

60.author: Hilali & Khan
60.text: (The followers of the misleaders will say): "Nay, you (too)! No welcome for you! It is you (misleaders) who brought this upon us (because you misled us in the world), so evil is this place to stay in!"

60.author: Itani
60.text: They will say, “But it is you! There is no welcome for you! It is you who brought it upon us! What a miserable end!”

60.author: Maududi
60.text: They will reply: “Rather, no welcome to you. (You will roast in Hell.) It is you who led us to this end. What an evil resort!”

60.author: Mubarakpuri
60.text: (The followers will say to those who misled): "Nay, you (too)! No welcome for you! It is you who brought this upon us, so evil is this place to stay in!"

60.author: Pickthall
60.text: They say: Nay, but you (misleaders), for you there is no word of welcome. Ye prepared this for us (by your misleading). Now hapless is the plight.

60.author: Qarai
60.text: They will say, ‘No, may wretchedness be your lot! You prepared this [hell] for us. What an evil abode!’

60.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
60.text: But they will say: 'No, it is you that has no welcome. It was you who brought it upon us, an evil place'

60.author: Saheeh International
60.text: They will say, "Nor you! No welcome for you. You, [our leaders], brought this upon us, and wretched is the settlement."

60.author: Sarwar
60.text: Their followers will say, "In fact, it is you who deserve condemnation. It was you who led us to hell, a terrible dwelling".

60.author: Shakir
60.text: They shall say: Nay! you-- no welcome to you: you did proffer it to us, so evil is the resting-place.

60.author: Wahiduddin Khan
60.text: They will say to them, "You are not welcome! It was you who brought this on us, an evil place to stay,"

60.author: Yusuf Ali
60.text: (The followers shall cry to the misleaders:) "Nay, ye (too)! No welcome for you! It is ye who have brought this upon us! Now evil is (this) place to stay in!"

60.author: Transliteration
60.text: Qaloo bal antum la marhaban bikum antum qaddamtumoohu lana fabisa alqararu

61.author: Ahmed Ali
61.text: They will say: "O Lord, give him who has brought this upon us two times more the torment of Hell;"

61.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
61.text: They say, “Our Lord! Whoever has brought this calamity upon us – double the punishment of the fire for him!”

61.author: Arberry
61.text: They say, 'Our Lord, whoso forwarded this for us, give him a double chastisement in the Fire!'

61.author: Daryabadi
61.text: They Will say: our Lord! whosever hath brought this upon us, - Unto him increase doubly the torment of the Fire.

61.author: Hilali & Khan
61.text: They will say: "Our Lord! Whoever brought this upon us, add to him a double torment in the Fire!"

61.author: Itani
61.text: They will say, “Our Lord, whoever brought this upon us, give him double torment in the Fire.”

61.author: Maududi
61.text: They will say: “Our Lord, give twofold punishment in the Fire to him who has led us to this.”

61.author: Mubarakpuri
61.text: They will say: "Our Lord! Whoever brought this upon us, add to him a double torment in the Fire!"

61.author: Pickthall
61.text: They say: Our Lord! Whoever did prepare this for us, oh, give him double portion of the Fire!

61.author: Qarai
61.text: They will say, ‘Our Lord! Whoever has prepared this for us, double his punishment in the Fire!’

61.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
61.text: They will say: 'Our Lord, give those who brought this upon us double the punishment of the Fire'

61.author: Saheeh International
61.text: They will say, "Our Lord, whoever brought this upon us - increase for him double punishment in the Fire."

61.author: Sarwar
61.text: They will continue saying, "Lord, double the torment of fire for those who led us into this.

61.author: Shakir
61.text: They shall say: Our Lord! whoever prepared it first for us, add Thou to him a double chastisement in the fire.

61.author: Wahiduddin Khan
61.text: adding, "Our Lord, give double punishment to those who brought this upon us."

61.author: Yusuf Ali
61.text: They will say: "Our Lord! whoever brought this upon us,- Add to him a double Penalty in the Fire!"

61.author: Transliteration
61.text: Qaloo rabbana man qaddama lana hatha fazidhu AAathaban diAAfan fee alnnari

62.author: Ahmed Ali
62.text: And will add: "O what has happened to us that we do not see the men we counted among the wicked.

62.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
62.text: And they say, “What is the matter with us that we do not see the men whom we thought were evil?”

62.author: Arberry
62.text: They say, 'How is it with us, that we do not see men here that we counted among the wicked?

62.author: Daryabadi
62.text: And they will Say: what aileth us that we behold not men whom we were wont to count among the evil ones?

62.author: Hilali & Khan
62.text: And they will say: "What is the matter with us that we see not men whom we used to count among the bad ones?"

62.author: Itani
62.text: And they will say, “What is it with us that we do not see men we used to count among the wicked?

62.author: Maududi
62.text: They will say to one another: “But why do we not see those whom we considered him among the wicked?

62.author: Mubarakpuri
62.text: And they will say: "What is the matter with us that we see not men whom we used to count among the bad ones"

62.author: Pickthall
62.text: And they say: What aileth us that we behold not men whom we were wont to count among the wicked?

62.author: Qarai
62.text: And they will say, ‘Why is it that we do not see [here] men whom we used to count among the bad ones,

62.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
62.text: And they will say: 'Why do we not see the men that we counted as being among the wicked in here?

62.author: Saheeh International
62.text: And they will say, "Why do we not see men whom we used to count among the worst?

62.author: Sarwar
62.text: But why is it that we cannot see men whom we had considered as wicked

62.author: Shakir
62.text: And they shall say: What is the matter with us that we do not see men whom we used to count among the vicious?

62.author: Wahiduddin Khan
62.text: And they will say, "How is it that we do not see [here any of the] men whom we used to count among the wicked,

62.author: Yusuf Ali
62.text: And they will say: "What has happened to us that we see not men whom we used to number among the bad ones?

62.author: Transliteration
62.text: Waqaloo ma lana la nara rijalan kunna naAAudduhum mina alashrari

63.author: Ahmed Ali
63.text: Did we laugh at them (for nothing), or our eyes fail to pick them out?"

63.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
63.text: “Did we mock at them or did our eyes turn away from them?”

63.author: Arberry
63.text: What, did we take them for a laughing-stock? Or have our eyes swerved away from them?'

63.author: Daryabadi
63.text: Took we them so unjustly for a butt of mockery, or are they deluding our eyes?

63.author: Hilali & Khan
63.text: Did we take them as an object of mockery, or have (our) eyes failed to perceive them?"

63.author: Itani
63.text: Did we take them for mockery, or have our eyes swerved from them?

63.author: Maududi
63.text: Is it that we mistakenly made fun of them; or have they disappeared from our sight?”

63.author: Mubarakpuri
63.text: "Did we take them as an object of mockery, or have (our) eyes failed to perceive them"

63.author: Pickthall
63.text: Did we take them (wrongly) for a laughing-stock, or have our eyes missed them?

63.author: Qarai
63.text: ridiculing them, or do [our] eyes miss them [here]?’

63.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
63.text: Have we taken them in mockery? Or, have our eyes swerved from them?'

63.author: Saheeh International
63.text: Is it [because] we took them in ridicule, or has [our] vision turned away from them?"

63.author: Sarwar
63.text: and whom we mocked? Have they been rescued or can our eyes not find them?"

63.author: Shakir
63.text: Was it that we (only) took them in scorn, or have our eyes (now) turned aside from them?

63.author: Wahiduddin Khan
63.text: [and] whom we made the target of our derision? Or are they here, and our eyes have missed them?"

63.author: Yusuf Ali
63.text: "Did we treat them (as such) in ridicule, or have (our) eyes failed to perceive them?"

63.author: Transliteration
63.text: Attakhathnahum sikhriyyan am zaghat AAanhumu alabsaru

64.author: Ahmed Ali
64.text: This contending of the inmates of Hell will surely be real.

64.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
64.text: Indeed this is really true – the people of the hell quarrelling among themselves.

64.author: Arberry
64.text: Surely that is true -- the disputing of the inhabitants of the Fire.

64.author: Daryabadi
64.text: Verily this is the very truth: the wrangling of the fellows of the Fire!

64.author: Hilali & Khan
64.text: Verily, that is the very truth, the mutual dispute of the people of the Fire!

64.author: Itani
64.text: This is certainly true—the feuding of the people of the Fire.

64.author: Maududi
64.text: Verily all this is true. This is how the inmates of the Fire will dispute among themselves.

64.author: Mubarakpuri
64.text: Verily, that is the very truth -- the mutual dispute of the people of the Fire!

64.author: Pickthall
64.text: Lo! that is very truth: the wrangling of the dwellers in the Fire.

64.author: Qarai
64.text: That is indeed a true account of the contentions of the inmates of the Fire.

64.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
64.text: Surely that is true the disputing of the inhabitants of the Fire

64.author: Saheeh International
64.text: Indeed, that is truth - the quarreling of the people of the Fire.

64.author: Sarwar
64.text: Such disputes will certainly take place among the dwellers of hell fire.

64.author: Shakir
64.text: That most surely is the truth: the contending one with another of the inmates of the fire.

64.author: Wahiduddin Khan
64.text: All this is certainly true -- the inhabitants of the Fire will blame one another in this way.

64.author: Yusuf Ali
64.text: Truly that is just and fitting,- the mutual recriminations of the People of the Fire!

64.author: Transliteration
64.text: Inna thalika lahaqqun takhasumu ahli alnnari

65.author: Ahmed Ali
65.text: Say: "I am only a warner, and there is no other god but God, the one, the omnipotent,

65.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
65.text: Proclaim (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “I am purely a Herald of Warning – and there is no God except Allah, the One, the All Dominant.”

65.author: Arberry
65.text: Say: 'I am only a warner. There is not any god but God, the One, the Omnipotent,

65.author: Daryabadi
65.text: Say thou: I am but warner, and there is no god but Allah, the One, the Subduer.

65.author: Hilali & Khan
65.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "I am only a warner and there is no Ilah (God) except Allah (none has the right to be worshipped but Allah) the One, the Irresistible,

65.author: Itani
65.text: Say, “I am only a warner, and there is no god except God—the One, the Conqueror.

65.author: Maududi
65.text: Tell them, (O Prophet): “I am nothing but a warner. There is no deity but Allah, the One, the Supreme,

65.author: Mubarakpuri
65.text: Say: "I am only a warner and there is no God (worthy of worship) except Allah the One, the Irresistible,"

65.author: Pickthall
65.text: Say (unto them, O Muhammad): I am only a warner, and there is no Allah save Allah, the One, the Absolute,

65.author: Qarai
65.text: Say, ‘I am just a warner, and there is no god except Allah, the One, the All-paramount,

65.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
65.text: Say: 'I am only a warner. There is no god except Allah, the One, the Conqueror,

65.author: Saheeh International
65.text: Say, [O Muhammad], "I am only a warner, and there is not any deity except Allah, the One, the Prevailing.

65.author: Sarwar
65.text: (Muhammad) say, "I am only a warner. The only Lord is God, the Almighty.

65.author: Shakir
65.text: Say: I am only a warner, and there is no god but Allah, the One, the Subduer (of all):

65.author: Wahiduddin Khan
65.text: Say, [Prophet], "I am only a warner. There is no god but God, the One, the All-Powerful,

65.author: Yusuf Ali
65.text: Say: "Truly am I a Warner: no god is there but the one Allah, Supreme and Irresistible,-

65.author: Transliteration
65.text: Qul innama ana munthirun wama min ilahin illa Allahu alwahidu alqahharu

66.author: Ahmed Ali
66.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that lies between them, all-mighty, all-forgiving."

66.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
66.text: “Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them – the Almighty, the Oft Forgiving.”

66.author: Arberry
66.text: Lord of the heavens and earth, and of what between them is, the All-mighty, the All-forgiving.'

66.author: Daryabadi
66.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatsoever is in- between the twain, the Mighty, the Forgiver.

66.author: Hilali & Khan
66.text: "The Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them, the All-Mighty, the Oft-Forgiving."

66.author: Itani
66.text: The Lord of the heavens and the earth, and everything between them; the Mighty, the Forgiver.”

66.author: Maududi
66.text: the Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that is in between them, the Most Mighty, the Most Forgiving.”

66.author: Mubarakpuri
66.text: "The Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them, the Almighty, the Oft-Forgiving."

66.author: Pickthall
66.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them, the Mighty, the Pardoning.

66.author: Qarai
66.text: the Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, the All-mighty, the All-forgiver.’

66.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
66.text: the Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them; the Almighty, the Forgiving'

66.author: Saheeh International
66.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, the Exalted in Might, the Perpetual Forgiver."

66.author: Sarwar
66.text: He is the Lord of the heavens, the earth, and all that is between them, the Majestic and All-forgiving".

66.author: Shakir
66.text: The Lord of the heavens and the earth and what is between them, the Mighty, the most Forgiving.

66.author: Wahiduddin Khan
66.text: Lord of the heavens and earth and everything between them, the Almighty, the Most Forgiving."

66.author: Yusuf Ali
66.text: "The Lord of the heavens and the earth, and all between,- Exalted in Might, able to enforce His Will, forgiving again and again."

66.author: Transliteration
66.text: Rabbu alssamawati waalardi wama baynahuma alAAazeezu alghaffaru

67.author: Daryabadi
67.text: Say thou: it is a tiding mighty.

67.author: Hilali & Khan
67.text: Say: "That (this Quran) is a great news,

67.author: Itani
67.text: Say, “It is a message of great importance.

67.author: Maududi
67.text: Say: “This is a tiding of tremendous import

67.author: Sarwar
67.text: Say, "It (facts about the supremacy of God) is the greatest message,

67.author: Shakir
67.text: Say: It is a message of importance,

67.author: Yusuf Ali
67.text: Say: "That is a Message Supreme (above all),-

68.author: Ahmed Ali
68.text: To which you pay no heed.

68.author: Arberry
68.text: from which you are turning away.

68.author: Itani
68.text: From which you are turning away.

68.author: Maududi
68.text: from which you are turning away.”

68.author: Sarwar
68.text: but you have turned away from it.

68.author: Shakir
68.text: (And) you are turning aside from it:

69.author: Ahmed Ali
69.text: I had no knowledge of the higher Assembly when they discussed it among themselves.

69.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
69.text: “What did I know of the heavenly world, when the angels had disputed.”

69.author: Arberry
69.text: I had no knowledge of the High Council when they disputed.

69.author: Daryabadi
69.text: I had no knowledge of the chiefs on high when they disputed;

69.author: Hilali & Khan
69.text: "I had no knowledge of the chiefs (angels) on high when they were disputing and discussing (about the creation of Adam).

69.author: Itani
69.text: I have no knowledge of the Highest Assembly as they dispute.

69.author: Maududi
69.text: (Tell them): “I had no knowledge of the High Council when they were disputing.

69.author: Mubarakpuri
69.text: "I had no knowledge of the chiefs (angels) on high when they were disputing and discussing."

69.author: Pickthall
69.text: I had no knowledge of the Highest Chiefs when they disputed;

69.author: Qarai
69.text: I have no knowledge of the Supernal Elite when they contend.

69.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
69.text: I had no knowledge of the High Assembly's dispute.

69.author: Saheeh International
69.text: I had no knowledge of the exalted assembly [of angels] when they were disputing [the creation of Adam].

69.author: Sarwar
69.text: I have no knowledge of the dispute among the angels (concerning their attitude towards Adam).

69.author: Shakir
69.text: I had no knowledge of the exalted chiefs when they contended:

69.author: Wahiduddin Khan
69.text: I had no knowledge of the Exalted Assembly when they argued [against the creation of man]:

69.author: Yusuf Ali
69.text: "No knowledge have I of the Chiefs on high, when they discuss (matters) among themselves.

69.author: Transliteration
69.text: Ma kana liya min AAilmin bialmalai alaAAla ith yakhtasimoona

70.author: Ahmed Ali
70.text: Only this has been revealed to me that I am a distinct warner.

70.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
70.text: “I receive only the divine revelations, that I am purely a clear Herald of Warning.”

70.author: Arberry
70.text: This alone is revealed to me, that I am only a clear warner.'

70.author: Daryabadi
70.text: Naught is revealed Unto me except that I am a warner manifest.

70.author: Hilali & Khan
70.text: "Only this has been inspired to me, that I am a plain warner."

70.author: Itani
70.text: It is only revealed to me that I am a clear warner.”

70.author: Maududi
70.text: I am told (about matters) by means of revelation only because I am a clear warner.”

70.author: Mubarakpuri
70.text: "Only this has been revealed to me, that I am a plain warner."

70.author: Pickthall
70.text: It is revealed unto me only that I may be a plain warner.

70.author: Qarai
70.text: All that is revealed to me is that I am just a manifest warner.’

70.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
70.text: This alone is revealed to me, I am only a clear warner'

70.author: Saheeh International
70.text: It has not been revealed to me except that I am a clear warner."

70.author: Sarwar
70.text: I have only received revelation to give you plain warning.

70.author: Shakir
70.text: Naught is revealed to me save that I am a plain warner.

70.author: Wahiduddin Khan
70.text: it has only been revealed to me that I am a plain warner."

70.author: Yusuf Ali
70.text: 'Only this has been revealed to me: that I am to give warning plainly and publicly."

70.author: Transliteration
70.text: In yooha ilayya illa annama ana natheerun mubeenun

71.author: Ahmed Ali
71.text: When your Lord said to the angels: "I am going to create a man from clay;

71.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
71.text: (Remember) When your Lord said to the angels, “I will create a human from clay.” –

71.author: Arberry
71.text: When thy Lord said to the angels, 'See, I am creating a mortal of a clay.

71.author: Daryabadi
71.text: Recall what time thy Lord said Unto the angels: verily I am about to create a human being from clay;

71.author: Hilali & Khan
71.text: (Remember) when your Lord said to the angels: "Truly, I am going to create man from clay".

71.author: Itani
71.text: Your Lord said to the angels, “I am creating a human being from clay.

71.author: Maududi
71.text: When your Lord said to the angels: “Verily I am creating a human being from clay.

71.author: Mubarakpuri
71.text: (Remember) when your Lord said to the angels: "Truly, I am going to create man from clay."

71.author: Pickthall
71.text: When thy Lord said unto the angels: Lo! I am about to create a mortal out of mire,

71.author: Qarai
71.text: When your Lord said to the angels, ‘Indeed I am about to create a human being out of clay.

71.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
71.text: When your Lord said to the angels: 'I am creating a human from clay,

71.author: Saheeh International
71.text: [So mention] when your Lord said to the angels, "Indeed, I am going to create a human being from clay.

71.author: Sarwar
71.text: When your Lord told the angels, "I will create a mortal out of clay,

71.author: Shakir
71.text: When your Lord said to the angels; Surely I am going to create a mortal from dust:

71.author: Wahiduddin Khan
71.text: Your Lord said to the angels, "I am about to create a human being out of clay;

71.author: Yusuf Ali
71.text: Behold, thy Lord said to the angels: "I am about to create man from clay:

71.author: Transliteration
71.text: Ith qala rabbuka lilmalaikati innee khaliqun basharan min teenin

72.author: Ahmed Ali
72.text: And when I have made him and have breathed into him of My spirit. Fall down in homage before him."

72.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
72.text: “So when I have perfected him and breathed into him a spirit from Myself, (you all) fall down before him in prostration.”

72.author: Arberry
72.text: When I have shaped him, and breathed My spirit in him, fall you down, bowing before him!'

72.author: Daryabadi
72.text: Then when I have formed him and breathed into him of My spirit, fall down before him prostrate.

72.author: Hilali & Khan
72.text: So when I have fashioned him and breathed into him (his) soul created by Me, then you fall down prostrate to him."

72.author: Itani
72.text: When I have formed him, and breathed into him of My spirit, fall prostrate before him.

72.author: Maududi
72.text: After I have created him and breathed into him of My spirit, fall you down, prostrating yourselves to him.”

72.author: Mubarakpuri
72.text: "So, when I have fashioned him and breathed into him (his) soul created by Me, then you fall down prostrate to him."

72.author: Pickthall
72.text: And when I have fashioned him and breathed into him of My Spirit, then fall down before him prostrate,

72.author: Qarai
72.text: So when I have proportioned him and breathed into him of My spirit, then fall down in prostration before him.’

72.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
72.text: after I have shaped him and breathed of My spirit (I created) into him, fall down prostrate before him'

72.author: Saheeh International
72.text: So when I have proportioned him and breathed into him of My [created] soul, then fall down to him in prostration."

72.author: Sarwar
72.text: and when I give it proper shape and blow My spirit into it, bow down in prostration to him,"

72.author: Shakir
72.text: So when I have made him complete and breathed into him of My spirit, then fall down making obeisance to him.
