72.author: Wahiduddin Khan
72.text: and when I have formed him fully and breathed My spirit into him, prostrate yourselves before him."

72.author: Yusuf Ali
72.text: "When I have fashioned him (in due proportion) and breathed into him of My spirit, fall ye down in obeisance unto him."

72.author: Transliteration
72.text: Faitha sawwaytuhu wanafakhtu feehi min roohee faqaAAoo lahu sajideena

73.author: Ahmed Ali
73.text: Then the angels bowed before him in a body,

73.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
73.text: So all the angels prostrated, every one, without exception.

73.author: Arberry
73.text: Then the angels bowed themselves all together,

73.author: Daryabadi
73.text: The angels prostrated themselves, all of them.

73.author: Hilali & Khan
73.text: So the angels prostrated themselves, all of them:

73.author: Itani
73.text: So the angels fell prostrate, all of them.

73.author: Maududi
73.text: Then the angels, all of them, prostrated themselves before Adam

73.author: Mubarakpuri
73.text: So, the angels prostrated themselves, all of them,

73.author: Pickthall
73.text: The angels fell down prostrate, every one,

73.author: Qarai
73.text: Thereat the angels prostrated, all of them together,

73.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
73.text: So all the angels prostrated themselves

73.author: Saheeh International
73.text: So the angels prostrated - all of them entirely.

73.author: Sarwar
73.text: all the angel then prostrated themselves

73.author: Shakir
73.text: And the angels did obeisance, all of them,

73.author: Wahiduddin Khan
73.text: Thereupon the angels prostrated themselves, all of them together,

73.author: Yusuf Ali
73.text: So the angels prostrated themselves, all of them together:

74.author: Ahmed Ali
74.text: Except Iblis. He was filled with pride and turned an unbeliever.

74.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
74.text: Except Iblis; he was proud and was, from the beginning, a disbeliever.

74.author: Arberry
74.text: save Iblis; he waxed proud, and was one of the unbelievers.

74.author: Daryabadi
74.text: Not so lblis. He grew stiffnecked and became of the infidels.

74.author: Hilali & Khan
74.text: Except Iblis (Satan) he was proud and was one of the disbelievers.

74.author: Itani
74.text: Except for Satan. He was too proud, and one of the faithless.

74.author: Maududi
74.text: except Iblis. He waxed proud and became one of the unbelievers.

74.author: Mubarakpuri
74.text: Except Iblis, he was proud and was one of the disbelievers.

74.author: Pickthall
74.text: Saving Iblis; he was scornful and became one of the disbelievers.

74.author: Qarai
74.text: but not Iblis; he acted arrogantly and he was one of the faithless.

74.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
74.text: except iblis (satan, the father of the jinn), he became too proud, for he was one of the unbelievers.

74.author: Saheeh International
74.text: Except Iblees; he was arrogant and became among the disbelievers.

74.author: Sarwar
74.text: except Iblis who puffed himself up with pride and became a disbeliever.

74.author: Shakir
74.text: But not Iblis: he was proud and he was one of the unbelievers.

74.author: Wahiduddin Khan
74.text: but not Satan, who was too proud. He became one of those who deny the truth.

74.author: Yusuf Ali
74.text: Not so Iblis: he was haughty, and became one of those who reject Faith.

74.author: Transliteration
74.text: Illa ibleesa istakbara wakana mina alkafireena

75.author: Ahmed Ali
75.text: Said (God): "O Iblis, what hindered you from adoring what I created by My own authority? Are you too proud, or too high and mighty?"

75.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
75.text: Said Allah, “O Iblis! What prevented you from prostrating before one whom I have created with My hands*? Have you become proud or were you haughty from the beginning?” (Used as a metaphor).

75.author: Arberry
75.text: Said He, 'Iblis, what prevented thee to bow thyself before that I created with My own hands? Hast thou waxed proud, or art thou of the lofty ones?'

75.author: Daryabadi
75.text: Allah said: O Iblis! what preventeth thee from prostrating thyself before that which have created with both my hands? Becomest thou stiff-necked, or art thou of the exalted ones?

75.author: Hilali & Khan
75.text: (Allah) said: "O Iblis (Satan)! What prevents you from prostrating yourself to one whom I have created with Both My Hands. Are you too proud (to fall prostrate to Adam) or are you one of the high exalted?"

75.author: Itani
75.text: He said, “O Satan, what prevented you from prostrating before what I created with My Own hands? Are you too proud, or were you one of the exalted?”

75.author: Maududi
75.text: The Lord said: “O Iblis, what prevented you from prostrating yourself before him whom I created of My Two Hands. Are you waxing proud, or fancy yourself to be too exalted?”

75.author: Mubarakpuri
75.text: (Allah) said: "O Iblis! What prevents you from prostrating yourself to one whom I have created with Both My Hands. Are you too proud or are you one of the high exalted"

75.author: Pickthall
75.text: He said: O Iblis! What hindereth thee from falling prostrate before that which I have created with both My hands? Art thou too proud or art thou of the high exalted?

75.author: Qarai
75.text: He said, ‘O Iblis! What keeps you from prostrating before that which I have created with My [own] two hands? Are you arrogant, or are you one of the exalted ones?’

75.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
75.text: He (Allah) said: 'iblis, what prevented you from prostrating yourself towards that which I have created with My Hands? Have you become too proud, or areyou among the grand'

75.author: Saheeh International
75.text: [Allah] said, "O Iblees, what prevented you from prostrating to that which I created with My hands? Were you arrogant [then], or were you [already] among the haughty?"

75.author: Sarwar
75.text: The Lord said, "Iblis, what prevented you from prostrating before what I have created with My own hands? Was it because of your pride or are you truly exalted?"

75.author: Shakir
75.text: He said: O Iblis! what prevented you that you should do obeisance to him whom I created with My two hands? Are you proud or are you of the exalted ones?

75.author: Wahiduddin Khan
75.text: God said, "Satan, what prevented you from prostrating yourself to what I created with My own Hands? Were you overcome by arrogance, or are you of those who think [only] of themselves as exalted?"

75.author: Yusuf Ali
75.text: (Allah) said: "O Iblis! What prevents thee from prostrating thyself to one whom I have created with my hands? Art thou haughty? Or art thou one of the high (and mighty) ones?"

75.author: Transliteration
75.text: Qala ya ibleesu ma manaAAaka an tasjuda lima khalaqtu biyadayya astakbarta am kunta mina alAAaleena

76.author: Ahmed Ali
76.text: He said: "I am better than he. You created me from fire, and him from clay."

76.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
76.text: Said Iblis, “I am better than him; You made me from fire, and You have created him from clay!”

76.author: Arberry
76.text: Said he, 'I am better than he; Thou createdst me of fire, and him Thou createdst of clay.'

76.author: Daryabadi
76.text: He said: I am better than he: me Thou hast created of fire, and him Thou hast created of clay.

76.author: Hilali & Khan
76.text: [Iblis (Satan)] said: "I am better than he, You created me from fire, and You created him from clay."

76.author: Itani
76.text: He said, “I am better than he; You created me from fire, and You created him from clay.”

76.author: Maududi
76.text: He replied: “I am nobler than he. You created me from fire and created him from clay.”

76.author: Mubarakpuri
76.text: [Iblis] said: "I am better than he. You created me from fire, and You created him from clay."

76.author: Pickthall
76.text: He said: I am better than him. Thou createdst me of fire, whilst him Thou didst create of clay.

76.author: Qarai
76.text: ‘I am better than him,’ he said. ‘You created me from fire and You created him from clay.’

76.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
76.text: He (satan) replied: 'I am better than he. You created me from fire, and You created him from clay'

76.author: Saheeh International
76.text: He said, "I am better than him. You created me from fire and created him from clay."

76.author: Sarwar
76.text: He said, "I am better than him. You have created me from fire and him out of clay".

76.author: Shakir
76.text: He said: I am better than he; Thou hast created me of fire, and him Thou didst create of dust.

76.author: Wahiduddin Khan
76.text: Satan replied, "I am better than him. You created me from fire, but You created him from clay."

76.author: Yusuf Ali
76.text: (Iblis) said: "I am better than he: thou createdst me from fire, and him thou createdst from clay."

76.author: Transliteration
76.text: Qala ana khayrun minhu khalaqtanee min narin wakhalaqtahu min teenin

77.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
77.text: He said, “Therefore exit from heaven, for you have been outcast.” (To disrespect the Prophets – peace and blessings be upon them – is blasphemy.)

77.author: Arberry
77.text: Said He, 'Then go thou forth hence; thou art accursed.

77.author: Daryabadi
77.text: Allah said: get thee forth therefrom verify thou art driven away.

77.author: Hilali & Khan
77.text: (Allah) said: "Then get out from here, for verily, you are outcast.

77.author: Itani
77.text: He said, “Then get out of here! You are an outcast!

77.author: Maududi
77.text: He said: “Get out of here; surely you are accursed,

77.author: Mubarakpuri
77.text: (Allah) said: "Then get out from here; for verily, you are outcast."

77.author: Pickthall
77.text: He said: Go forth from hence, for lo! thou art outcast,

77.author: Qarai
77.text: He said, ‘Begone hence, for you are indeed an outcast,

77.author: Saheeh International
77.text: [Allah] said, "Then get out of Paradise, for indeed, you are expelled.

77.author: Sarwar
77.text: The Lord said, "Get out of here. You deserve to be stoned!

77.author: Shakir
77.text: He said: Then get out of it, for surely you are driven away:

77.author: Yusuf Ali
77.text: (Allah) said: "Then get thee out from here: for thou art rejected, accursed.

78.author: Ahmed Ali
78.text: Upon you will be My damnation till the Day of Doom."

78.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
78.text: “And indeed My curse is upon you till the Day of Judgement.”

78.author: Arberry
78.text: Upon thee shall rest My curse, till the Day of Doom.'

78.author: Daryabadi
78.text: And verily My curse shall be on thee till the Day of Requital,

78.author: Hilali & Khan
78.text: "And verily!, My Curse is on you till the Day of Recompense."

78.author: Itani
78.text: And My curse will be upon you until the Day of Judgment.”

78.author: Maududi
78.text: and My curse shall remain upon you till the Day of Resurrection.”

78.author: Mubarakpuri
78.text: "And verily, My curse is on you till the Day of Recompense."

78.author: Pickthall
78.text: And lo! My curse is on thee till the Day of Judgment.

78.author: Qarai
78.text: and indeed My curse will be on you till the Day of Retribution.’

78.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
78.text: 'My curse shall rest on you until the Day of Recompense'

78.author: Saheeh International
78.text: And indeed, upon you is My curse until the Day of Recompense."

78.author: Sarwar
78.text: My condemnation will be with you until the Day of Judgment!"

78.author: Shakir
78.text: And surely My curse is on you to the day of judgment.

78.author: Wahiduddin Khan
78.text: My curse will remain upon you till the Day of Judgement!"

78.author: Yusuf Ali
78.text: "And My curse shall be on thee till the Day of Judgment."

78.author: Transliteration
78.text: Wainna AAalayka laAAnatee ila yawmi alddeeni

79.author: Ahmed Ali
79.text: He said: "O Lord, give me respite till the day the dead rise from their graves."

79.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
79.text: He said, “My Lord! Therefore give me respite till the day when all will be raised.”

79.author: Arberry
79.text: Said he, 'My Lord, respite me till the day they shall be raised.'

79.author: Daryabadi
79.text: He said: my Lord! respite me till the Day whereon they are raised up.

79.author: Hilali & Khan
79.text: [Iblis (Satan)] said: "My Lord! Give me then respite till the Day the (dead) are resurrected."

79.author: Itani
79.text: He said, “Lord, defer me until the Day they are resurrected.”

79.author: Maududi
79.text: Satan said: “My Lord, then grant me respite till the Day that they are raised up.”

79.author: Mubarakpuri
79.text: [Iblis] said: "My Lord! Give me then respite till the Day the (dead) are resurrected."

79.author: Pickthall
79.text: He said: My Lord! Reprieve me till the day when they are raised.

79.author: Qarai
79.text: He said, ‘My Lord! Respite me till the day they will be resurrected.’

79.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
79.text: He (satan) replied: 'Respite me my Lord till the Day of Resurrection'

79.author: Saheeh International
79.text: He said, "My Lord, then reprieve me until the Day they are resurrected."

79.author: Sarwar
79.text: He said, "Lord, grant me respite until the Day of Resurrection".

79.author: Shakir
79.text: He said: My Lord! then respite me to the day that they are raised.

79.author: Wahiduddin Khan
79.text: But Satan said, "My Lord, grant me respite until the Day of Resurrection,"

79.author: Yusuf Ali
79.text: (Iblis) said: "O my Lord! Give me then respite till the Day the (dead) are raised."

79.author: Transliteration
79.text: Qala rabbi faanthirnee ila yawmi yubAAathoona

80.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
80.text: Said Allah, “You are therefore among those given respite.”

80.author: Arberry
80.text: Said He, 'Thou art among the ones that are respited

80.author: Daryabadi
80.text: Allah said: verily, thou art of those respited.

80.author: Hilali & Khan
80.text: (Allah) said: "Verily! You are of those allowed respite

80.author: Itani
80.text: He said, “You are one of those deferred.

80.author: Maududi
80.text: He said: “You are of those who have been granted respite

80.author: Mubarakpuri
80.text: (Allah) said: "Verily, you are of those allowed respite,"

80.author: Pickthall
80.text: He said: Lo! thou art of those reprieved

80.author: Qarai
80.text: Said He, ‘You are indeed among the reprieved

80.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
80.text: He (Allah) said: 'You are among those that are respited

80.author: Saheeh International
80.text: [Allah] said, "So indeed, you are of those reprieved

80.author: Sarwar
80.text: The Lord said, "You will only be given a respite

80.author: Shakir
80.text: He said: Surely you are of the respited ones,

80.author: Wahiduddin Khan
80.text: so He said, "You are granted respite

81.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
81.text: “Until the time of the known day.”

81.author: Arberry
81.text: until the day of the known time.'

81.author: Daryabadi
81.text: Until the Day of the time appointed.

81.author: Hilali & Khan
81.text: "Till the Day of the time appointed."

81.author: Itani
81.text: Until the Day of the Time Appointed.”

81.author: Maududi
81.text: till the Day whose Hour I know.”

81.author: Mubarakpuri
81.text: "Till the Day of the time appointed."

81.author: Pickthall
81.text: Until the day of the time appointed.

81.author: Qarai
81.text: until the day of the known time.’

81.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
81.text: till the Day of the known time'

81.author: Saheeh International
81.text: Until the Day of the time well-known."

81.author: Shakir
81.text: Till the period of the time made known.

81.author: Yusuf Ali
81.text: "Till the Day of the Time Appointed."

82.author: Ahmed Ali
82.text: He said: "By Your authority, I will lead them astray,

82.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
82.text: He said, “Therefore, by oath of Your honour, I will surely mislead all of them.”

82.author: Arberry
82.text: Said he, 'Now, by Thy glory, I shall pervert them all together,

82.author: Daryabadi
82.text: He said: by Thy majesty, then, I shall surely seduce them, all.

82.author: Hilali & Khan
82.text: [Iblis (Satan)] said: "By Your Might, then I will surely mislead them all,

82.author: Itani
82.text: He said, “By Your majesty, I will seduce them all.

82.author: Maududi
82.text: (Iblis) said: “By Your glory, I shall mislead them all

82.author: Mubarakpuri
82.text: [Iblis] said: "By Your might, then I will surely mislead them all,"

82.author: Pickthall
82.text: He said: Then, by Thy might, I surely will beguile them every one,

82.author: Qarai
82.text: He said, ‘By Your might, I will surely pervert them,

82.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
82.text: He (satan) said: 'I swear by Your Might, that I will seduce all of them

82.author: Saheeh International
82.text: [Iblees] said, "By your might, I will surely mislead them all

82.author: Sarwar
82.text: He said, "By Your Glory, I shall seduce all of them (children of Adam).

82.author: Shakir
82.text: He said: Then by Thy Might I will surely make them live an evil life, all,

82.author: Wahiduddin Khan
82.text: He said, "By Your Honour, I will lead all of them astray,

82.author: Yusuf Ali
82.text: (Iblis) said: "Then, by Thy power, I will put them all in the wrong,-

83.author: Ahmed Ali
83.text: Other than the chosen ones among Your creatures."

83.author: Arberry
83.text: excepting those Thy servants among them that are sincere.'

83.author: Daryabadi
83.text: Save Thy bondmen among them Sincere.

83.author: Hilali & Khan
83.text: "Except Your chosen slaves amongst them (faithful, obedient, true believers of Islamic Monotheism)."

83.author: Itani
83.text: Except for your loyal servants among them.”

83.author: Maududi
83.text: except those of Your servants, the chosen ones from amongst them.”

83.author: Pickthall
83.text: Save Thy single-minded slaves among them.

83.author: Qarai
83.text: except Your exclusive servants among them.’

83.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
83.text: except those among them who are Your sincere worshipers'

83.author: Saheeh International
83.text: Except, among them, Your chosen servants."

83.author: Sarwar
83.text: except Your sincere servants among them".

83.author: Shakir
83.text: Except Thy servants from among them, the purified ones.

83.author: Wahiduddin Khan
83.text: except for those among them who are Your chosen servants."

83.author: Yusuf Ali
83.text: "Except Thy Servants amongst them, sincere and purified (by Thy Grace)."

84.author: Ahmed Ali
84.text: (God) said: "This is right by Me, and what I say is right.

84.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
84.text: Said Allah, “So this is the truth; and I speak only the truth.” –

84.author: Arberry
84.text: Said He, 'This is the truth, and the truth I say;

84.author: Daryabadi
84.text: Allah said: the truth is, and it is the truth that I speak, -

84.author: Hilali & Khan
84.text: (Allah) said: "The Truth is, and the Truth I say,

84.author: Itani
84.text: He said, “The truth is, and I say the truth.

84.author: Maududi
84.text: He (i.e. Allah) said: “This is the Truth -- and I only speak the Truth --

84.author: Mubarakpuri
84.text: (Allah) said: "The truth is -- the truth I say - "

84.author: Pickthall
84.text: He said: The Truth is, and the Truth I speak,

84.author: Qarai
84.text: Said He, ‘The truth is that—and I speak the truth—

84.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
84.text: He (Allah) said: 'This is the truth, and I speak the truth:

84.author: Saheeh International
84.text: [Allah] said, "The truth [is My oath], and the truth I say -

84.author: Sarwar
84.text: The Lord said, "I swear by the Truth - and I speak the Truth -

84.author: Shakir
84.text: He said: The truth then is and the truth do I speak:

84.author: Wahiduddin Khan
84.text: God said, "This is the truth, I speak only the truth,

84.author: Yusuf Ali
84.text: (Allah) said: "Then it is just and fitting- and I say what is just and fitting-

85.author: Ahmed Ali
85.text: I will fill up Hell with you together with those who follow you."

85.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
85.text: “That I will fill hell with you and with those among them who follow you, all together.”

85.author: Arberry
85.text: I shall assuredly fill Gehenna with thee, and with whosoever of them follows thee, all together.'

85.author: Daryabadi
85.text: That I shall fill Hell with thee and such of them as shall follow thee, all together.

85.author: Hilali & Khan
85.text: That I will fill Hell with you [Iblis (Satan)] and those of them (mankind) that follow you, together."

85.author: Itani
85.text: I will fill Hell with you, and with every one of them who follows you.”

85.author: Maududi
85.text: I will certainly fill the Gehenna with you and with all those among them who follow you.”

85.author: Mubarakpuri
85.text: "That I will fill Hell with you and those of them (mankind) that follow you, together."

85.author: Pickthall
85.text: That I shall fill hell with thee and with such of them as follow thee, together.

85.author: Qarai
85.text: I will surely fill hell with you and all those who follow you.’

85.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
85.text: I shall certainly fill Gehenna with you and every one of them that follows you'

85.author: Saheeh International
85.text: [That] I will surely fill Hell with you and those of them that follow you all together."

85.author: Sarwar
85.text: that I shall certainly fill hell with you and your followers all together".

85.author: Shakir
85.text: That I will most certainly fill hell with you and with those among them who follow you, all.

85.author: Wahiduddin Khan
85.text: I will fill up Hell with you and every one of them who follows you."

85.author: Yusuf Ali
85.text: "That I will certainly fill Hell with thee and those that follow thee,- every one."

85.author: Transliteration
85.text: Laamlaanna jahannama minka wamimman tabiAAaka minhum ajmaAAeena

86.author: Ahmed Ali
86.text: Say: "I do not ask any compensation of you for it, nor am I a specious pretender.

86.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
86.text: Say (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) “I do not ask any fee from you for the Qur’an, and I am not a fabricator.”

86.author: Arberry
86.text: Say: 'I ask of you no wage for it, neither am I of those who take things upon themselves.

86.author: Daryabadi
86.text: Say thou: ask of you for it no hire, nor am I of the affecters.

86.author: Hilali & Khan
86.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "No wage do I ask of you for this (the Quran), nor am I one of the Mutakallifun (those who pretend and fabricate things which do not exist).

86.author: Itani
86.text: Say, “I ask of you no wage for this, and I am not a pretender.

86.author: Maududi
86.text: (O Prophet), tell them: “I do not ask you for any recompense for the performance of this task; nor am I given to affectation.

86.author: Mubarakpuri
86.text: Say: "No wage do I ask of you for this, nor am I one of the Mutakallifin."

86.author: Pickthall
86.text: Say (O Muhammad, unto mankind): I ask of you no fee for this, and I am no simulating.

86.author: Qarai
86.text: Say, ‘I do not ask you any reward for it, and I am no impostor.

86.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
86.text: Say (Prophet Muhammad): 'For this I ask of you no wage, and I am not of those who take things upon themselves.

86.author: Saheeh International
86.text: Say, [O Muhammad], "I do not ask you for the Qur'an any payment, and I am not of the pretentious

86.author: Sarwar
86.text: (Muhammad), say, "I do not ask any reward for my preaching to you for I am not a pretender.

86.author: Shakir
86.text: Say: I do not ask you for any reward for it; nor am I of those who affect:

86.author: Wahiduddin Khan
86.text: Say, "I do not ask you for any recompense for this, nor am I a man of false pretentions:

86.author: Yusuf Ali
86.text: Say: "No reward do I ask of you for this (Qur'an), nor am I a pretender.

86.author: Transliteration
86.text: Qul ma asalukum AAalayhi min ajrin wama ana mina almutakallifeena

87.author: Ahmed Ali
87.text: This is only a warning for mankind.

87.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
87.text: “It is not but an advice for the entire world.”

87.author: Arberry
87.text: It is nothing but a reminder unto all beings,

87.author: Daryabadi
87.text: It is naught but an admonition Unto the worlds.

87.author: Hilali & Khan
87.text: "It (this Quran) is only a Reminder for all the 'Alamin (mankind and jinns).

87.author: Itani
87.text: It is but a reminder to mankind.

87.author: Maududi
87.text: This is nothing but an Admonition for all people the world over.

87.author: Mubarakpuri
87.text: "It is only a Reminder for all the creatures."

87.author: Pickthall
87.text: Lo! it is naught else than a reminder for all peoples

87.author: Qarai
87.text: It is just a reminder for all the nations,

87.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
87.text: This is nothing else but a reminder to all the worlds,

87.author: Saheeh International
87.text: It is but a reminder to the worlds.

87.author: Sarwar
87.text: It, (the Quran), is nothing but a reminder to you from the Lord of the Universe.

87.author: Shakir
87.text: It is nothing but a reminder to the nations;

87.author: Wahiduddin Khan
87.text: this is simply an admonition to mankind,

87.author: Yusuf Ali
87.text: "This is no less than a Message to (all) the Worlds.

88.author: Ahmed Ali
88.text: You will come to know its truth in time."

88.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
88.text: “And you will come to know of its tidings, after a while.”

88.author: Arberry
88.text: and you shall surely know its tiding after a while.'

88.author: Daryabadi
88.text: And ye shall surely come to know the truth thereof after a season.

88.author: Hilali & Khan
88.text: "And you shall certainly know the truth of it after a while.

88.author: Itani
88.text: And you will know its message after a while.”

88.author: Maududi
88.text: You will know the truth of the matter after a while.”

88.author: Mubarakpuri
88.text: "And you shall certainly know the truth of it after a while."

88.author: Pickthall
88.text: And ye will come in time to know the truth thereof.

88.author: Qarai
88.text: and you will surely learn its tidings in due time.’

88.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
88.text: and after a while you shall know its news'

88.author: Saheeh International
88.text: And you will surely know [the truth of] its information after a time."

88.author: Sarwar
88.text: You will certainly know its truthfulness after a certain time.

88.author: Shakir
88.text: And most certainly you will come to know about it after a time.

88.author: Wahiduddin Khan
88.text: you shall before long know its truth."

88.author: Yusuf Ali
88.text: "And ye shall certainly know the truth of it (all) after a while."
